issue_title: Accent-insensitive LIKE
issue_body:
Hi all--
we have several use-cases where we need a LIKE query to ignore accents (e.g. matching "a" and "á"). Is this possible already, or can it be made possible?

PostgreSQL (which all of these cases use) provides the unaccent-extension, so that I thought it might be useful to expose that, either as unaccent() operator for Expressions, or as an extension to the .like() operator as in: Field.like(..., ignore_accents=True).

Surely, unaccent needs to be switched on by "CREATE EXTENSION unaccent", which could happen e.g. during adapter instantiation, parametrized as unaccent=True.

How complicated would that be? How can it be done? Anyone who can assist, or give the right pointers?

Many thanks,
Dominic

P.S. Alternative to use REGEX already considered, and actually working, but not scalable enough.

issue_title: example notebook showing cartoframes and bokeh working together
since theano 0.9 support multi-gpu, does lasagne now support multi-gpu, especially for model paralleism?
Change theano.config.device.startswith("gpu") check
issue_body:
As discussed in https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/lasagne-users/AWbQ0vvJjrQ, the existing `theano.config.device.startswith("gpu")` checks are too strict -- Theano provides a second way to use a GPU, via `theano.sandbox.cuda.use`, and Lasagne wrongly prevents `lasagne.layers.dnn` to be imported in that case.

From https://groups.google.com/d/msg/lasagne-users/AWbQ0vvJjrQ/neQZc-vLBgAJ, it seems the most universal solution for now would be replacing it with `theano.sandbox.cuda.use.device_number >= 0`. We might want to check if there's a solution that also works in the new GPU backend, though.

In any case, this would require changes in the mocks for readthedocs as well.

issue_title: synchronous producer hangs when kafka is unreachable
issue_body:
*This issue was brought to my attention by Michael Kowalchik in the [mailing list](https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/pykafka-user/iyZlHj_NTDU)*

**Steps to reproduce**

* Run a synchronous producer pointing at a kafka cluster with a single node
* Kill the single kafka node while the producer is running
* Observe that the producer's main thread hangs and must be killed externally

The expected result is that the producer's main thread should raise a `SocketDisconnectedError` from `produce()`.

The following sequence of events causes this bug. When a broker is unavailable, `SocketDisconnectedError` is raised and caught. If there are no brokers available, [this call to _update()](https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/2.1.2/pykafka/producer.py#L366) raises an [exception](https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/2.1.2/pykafka/cluster.py#L286). This in turn [kills the worker thread](https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/2.1.2/pykafka/producer.py#L443), meaning that code after the `_update` call, including pushing the original `SocketDisconnectedError` onto the delivery reports queue, does not execute. On the main thread, `produce()` is still waiting for the [delivery report](https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/2.1.2/pykafka/producer.py#L268), which will never come because the worker has died.

Possible solutions include ignoring certain exception types from the `_update()` call, or catching any exception that happens between `to_retry` being populated and the delivery report being enqueued. I'd lean toward simply catching and warning about [this specific exception](https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/2.1.2/pykafka/cluster.py#L286), maybe giving it a more specific name in the process.

issue_title: Leaderless Topics Shouldn't Break KafkaClient
issue_body:
In [broker.py](https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/12f522870a32198f70a92ce543950c88b9f75565/pykafka/broker.py#L264-L266), if a topic has a missing partition leader, then [cluster initialization](https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/12f522870a32198f70a92ce543950c88b9f75565/pykafka/cluster.py#L154-L156) fails, raising an Exception. This behavior is no good. We should warn, but really only throw the exception if someone goes to read/write that topic/partition.

Also, we should add the topic/partition to those log messages, because it's pretty unhelpful right now.

issue_title: Parallelism hint not propagated to Flux YAML Topology
issue_body:
Python Topology DSL parallelism hints for spouts and bolts are not included in the Flux YAML DSL passed into Storm from a local sparse run invocation. This appears to be an oversight in the code, (see below), so this should be evident using any topology including the sample wordcount.

For example, the following bolt declaration in the Python DSL:

```py
    uniques_bolt = \
        UniquesBolt.spec(name=b'uniques_bolt',
                         inputs={redis_spout[b'events']: Grouping.SHUFFLE},
                         par=2)
```

Resulted in this YAML DSL:

```yaml
- className: org.apache.storm.flux.wrappers.bolts.FluxShellBolt
  configMethods:
  - args:
    - events
    - [event]
    name: setNamedStream
  constructorArgs:
  - [streamparse_run, bolts.uniques.UniquesBolt]
  id: uniques_bolt
```

There is obviously no parallelism hint included here. Looking at the log files generated by the running bolts, it was clear that there was only one executor running after invoking sparse run:

```
pystorm_events_uniques_bolt_12_4674.log
```

After a little digging, I patched streamparse/dsl/topology.py to include the parallelism hint:

```diff
# diff -u topology.py.orig topology.py
--- topology.py.orig	2017-04-28 10:01:40.645000000 -0400
+++ topology.py	2017-04-28 09:45:53.779000000 -0400
@@ -210,6 +210,7 @@
                             spec.outputs[output_stream].output_fields
                         ]
                     })
+            flux_dict['parallelism'] = spec.par
         else:
             if spec.component_object.serialized_java is not None:
                 raise TypeError('Flux does not support specifying serialized '
```

Afterwards, the YAML DSL looked like this:

```yaml
- className: org.apache.storm.flux.wrappers.bolts.FluxShellBolt
  configMethods:
  - args:
    - events
    - [event]
    name: setNamedStream
  constructorArgs:
  - [streamparse_run, bolts.uniques.UniquesBolt]
  id: uniques_bolt
  parallelism: 2
```

And the expected number of executor logs appeared:

```
pystorm_events_uniques_bolt_16_17514.log
pystorm_events_uniques_bolt_17_17534.log
```

I am not sure this patch is absolutely correct, but it illustrates the problem and a potential solution. For reference, we are using Streamparse 3.4.0 on Storm 1.1.0, (CentOS 7.2, Python 2.7).

I've selected for refactoring 8 lines of code which are duplicated in 2 file(s) ([1](https://github.com/PolyJIT/benchbuild/blob/52ec6f984169c158e9517fd6179ad4b6ded27eaf/benchbuild/experiments/ijpp.py#L187-L194), [2](https://github.com/PolyJIT/benchbuild/blob/52ec6f984169c158e9517fd6179ad4b6ded27eaf/benchbuild/experiments/ijpp.py#L164-L171), [3](https://github.com/PolyJIT/benchbuild/blob/52ec6f984169c158e9517fd6179ad4b6ded27eaf/benchbuild/experiments/polyjit.py#L27-L34)).  Addressing this will make our codebase more maintainable and improve [Better Code Hub](https://bettercodehub.com)'s **Write Code Once** guideline rating! 👍 

Here's the gist of this guideline:
- **Definition** 📖 
Do not copy code.
- **Why**❓ 
When code is copied, bugs need to be fixed in multiple places. This is both inefficient and a source of regression bugs.
- **How** 🔧 
Avoid duplication by never copy/pasting blocks of code and reduce duplication by extracting shared code, either to a new unit or introduce a superclass if the language permits.

You can find more info about this guideline in [Building Maintainable Software](http://shop.oreilly.com/product/0636920049159.do). 📖 
 
---- 
ℹ️ To know how many _other_ refactoring candidates need addressing to get a guideline compliant, select some by clicking on the 🔲  next to them. The risk profile below the candidates signals (✅) when it's enough! 🏁

issue_title: Do not accept invalid arguments to --remote-data
issue_body: For example, passing `--remote-data=foo` should cause an error as early as possible.

issue_title: No support for specifying output format
issue_body:
The `flip` program linked in the README supports flags for specifying the output format. Beyond flipping, this would support the additional use cases of:

* Clean/validate a file and keep the data format
* Flip mixed/unknown files into one type

This would be useful in build-time validation and when using CF templates from dependencies.

issue_title: Deploy code for a single function without deploying the whole stack?
issue_body:
What would be the best way to update the code of a single function without having to package and deploy the whole stack (which is getting quite slow as the app grows)? Basically, do the equivalent of this [AWS Deploy Function of Serverless framework](https://serverless.com/framework/docs/providers/aws/cli-reference/deploy-function/).

I was thinking about using the [update-function-code](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/lambda/update-function-code.html) of the lambda cli but not sure what the impact will be on the stack if it start updating some of its resources outside of the normal package->deploy process

issue_title: Internal transform failure with AWS::Serverless::Function
issue_body:
Hello. Just starting out with SAM, so maybe I'm missing something absolutely obvious to you.
I'm hitting this error during the deploy step: `Transform AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31 failed with: Internal transform failure.`

My Template:
```
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Transform: "AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31"
Resources:
   CPEBSSRole:
      Type: "AWS::IAM::Role"
      Properties:
         RoleName: { "Fn::Join": [ ".", [ {"Ref": "AWS::Region"}, "CPEBSSRole" ] ] }
         AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
               -
                  Effect: "Allow"
                  Principal:
                     Service:
                        - "lambda.amazonaws.com"
                  Action:
                     - "sts:AssumeRole"
   CPEBSSPolicy:
      Type: "AWS::IAM::Policy"
      DependsOn: "CPEBSSRole"
      Properties:
         PolicyName: CPEBSSPolicy
         # Groups | Roles | Users
         Roles: [ { "Ref": "CPEBSSRole" } ]
         PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
               -
                  Effect: "Allow"
                  Action:
                     - "iam:GetUser"
                  Resource: "*"
               -
                  Effect: "Allow"
                  Action:
                     - "ec2:CopySnapshot"
                     - "ec2:DescribeSnapshots"
                  Resource: "*"
               -
                  Effect: "Allow"
                  Action:
                     - "logs:CreateLogGroup"
                     - "logs:CreateLogStream"
                     - "logs:PutLogEvents"
                     - "logs:DescribeLogStreams"
                  Resource: "arn:aws:logs:*:*:*"
   # =========================================================
   CPEBSSFunction:
      Type: "AWS::Serverless::Function"
      DependsOn: "CPEBSSRole"
      Properties:
         FunctionName: cpebss
         Description: Copy EBS Snapshots
         Handler: cpebss.handler
         CodeUri: ./cpebss.py
         Runtime: python2.7
         Role: "arn:aws:iam::************:role/us-east-1.CPEBSSRole"
         # { "Fn::GetAtt": ["CPEBSSRole", "Arn"] }
```

If I chop off the part after the commented horizontal line (# ===...), I can
```
aws cloudformation package \
   --template-file cpebss_cfn.yaml \
   --s3-bucket mybucket \
   --output-template-file cfn_packaged.yaml

aws cloudformation deploy \
   --template-file cfn_packaged.yaml \
   --stack-name mystack \
   --capabilities CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM \
```

But with the CPEBSSFunction resource (the part after the comment line), the `deploy` command fails with:
```
Failed to create the changeset: Waiter ChangeSetCreateComplete failed: Waiter encountered a terminal failure state Status: FAILED. Reason: Transform AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31 failed with: Internal transform failure.
```

You can see I originally had a `Fn::GetAtt` for the function's Role, but have even tried with a hardcoded Role ARN (I blotted out the account ID while posting here) to no avail.

You're my only hope.

issue_title: Feature-request: Support Client app w/generated secret
issue_body:
So far it seems that the authentication flow of warrant only supports AWS Cognito clients without generated secrets. (Like the official AWS Javascript APIs). 

It would have been great if warrant in the future also added support for AWS Cognito clients with generated secrets as well.

issue_title: --deploy-hook not documented in certbot certonly docs
issue_body: `certbot --help certonly` does not explain that the `--deploy-hook` function can be used to run a deploy hook after issuing the certificate, and that it **also** configures the hook to run on renewals too (by adding it to `/etc/letsencrypt/renewal/example.com.conf` as a `renew_hook` option). This is very useful to know.

issue_title: Provide neither an e-mail or --register-unsafely flag
issue_body:
1. Run the client with the `certonly` or `run` subcommands and a fresh config.
2. When asked for an e-mail, provide one that passes the clients `safe_email` test but will fail when sent to boulder. I used `<keyboard mashing>@<keyboard mashing>.com`.
3. When asked for an e-mail again, cancel. The client will register without an e-mail address.

issue_title: '--allow-subset-of-names' issues new certificates even if validated domains didn't change

## My operating system is (include version):
Debian 9 Stretch, certbot 0.10.2

## I installed Certbot with (certbot-auto, OS package manager, pip, etc):
`apt install certbot` (from Debian 9 Stretch)

## I ran this command and it produced this output:

I requested a certificate for two domains `test1.example.org` and `test2.example.org`:

```
certbot certonly --webroot --webroot-path /var/www/html --cert-name testcert --domains test1.example.org,test2.example.org --keep-until-expiring --allow-subset-of-names --non-interactive --agree-tos

issue_title: "certbot certificates" should check validity with OpenSSL
issue_body: All sorts of weird things could have happened to the certs on disk to make them invalid, so it might be good for `certbot certificates` to run them through `openssl verify` or equivalent, and report any failures.

issue_title: Fix Nginx prompt
issue_body:
Saw the following prompt when using Nginx
```
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
No names were found in your configuration files.
You should specify ServerNames in your config files in order to allow for
accurate installation of your certificate.
If you do use the default vhost, you may specify the name manually. Would you
like to continue?
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
(Y)es/(N)o: 
```
`ServerName` is Apache specific and based on #3508, we don't want to allow people to use the default vhost.

issue_title: documentation: section about renewing structured below "Changing Certificates Domains"
issue_body:
Hi guys,

I know this is a website thingy, but the code for `using.html` is within this repo, so I'm putting the issue here too.

Something seems a little bit strange: the help text about renewing a certificate is an item structured *below* "Changing a Certificate’s Domains" in the documentation.

[In January of 2017](https://github.com/certbot/certbot/commit/bf7ce3130298016bdeb970e7c8e04cf319099969#diff-5f059e53687997d121c03d63d11f103bL259), the section "Renewing certificates" was demoted a level below the "head" section "Managing certificates". At the time, "Changing a Certificate’s Domains" was a section with the same level as renewing.

[But in March 2017](https://github.com/certbot/certbot/commit/df8b374916d2e76b2377def98a6ffcdd2a09b112), the "Changing a Certificate's Domains" section got leveled up.

From that moment on, if people are to search for "Renewing", they have to explicitly click on "Changing a Certificate's Domains" to see the renewing section in the menu.

The change in level of the heading seems unrelated to any other change. In the [PR itself](https://github.com/certbot/certbot/pull/4948) there's no mentioning of this.

Perhaps it was by accident and it slipped through?

issue_title: Alternative approach via stubfiles
issue_body:
This is not an issue. I just wanted to crosslink an approach with the same goal that might be of help for some use cases (and eventually save us all from doing work twice).

``TypeLogger`` from [pytypes](https://github.com/Stewori/pytypes) can observe types at runtime and then write a PEP-484 stubfile from that information.
All versions of ``typing`` are supported and it runs on Python 2.7, 3.3, 3.4, 3.5, 3.6, PyPy and Jython.
See https://github.com/Stewori/pytypes#usage-example-with-profiler.

The approach differs from pyannotate in that it does not write into existing code, but always creates an external PEP-484 conform stubfile that should be suitable for use with mypy (pytypes itself can use it for runtime typechecking).

A nice goody is that it can take existing type annotations into account and extend them by information acquired from runtime observations.
The tool supports OOP -- classes, inner classes, static methods, class methods and properties -- and automatically writes a proper import section for the types in use.

Disclaimer: It is not yet perfect and still in beta. Please file issues as the come up. Help is welcome!

issue_title: Merge multiple indices in one
issue_body:
Hi,

Is it possible to add support for merging multiple old indices in one.

In the case of having an index per day, after a couple of months, we are running into having a lot of openned indices and Elasticsearch becomes slow because of that.

It would be nice if we were able to merge the old indices by weeks, by months or by year.

By example, instead of having 60 openned indices (one per day) for the last 2 months, merging by weeks the indices older than 7 days would results in:
```
  7 openned indices for the last 7 days
+ 7 openned indices for the other 7 weeks
-----
  14 openned indices (instead of 60 indices)
```
This would really help for the memory management of Elasticsearch and will help for sure the response time while querying in Kibana.

Thanks,

David

issue_title: Major-ordering of 'delete', select-by-alias
issue_body:
As a kind of logical structuring, my organization prefixes each series name with an identifier for the owning project/group (e.g. 'common-apache-2016.02.23' for the 'apache' series, which belongs to group 'common'). When we want to select with curator a specific series, we're careful to name prefixes up to and including that dash character separating the date. We've recently switched our logging over from a nearly identical naming scheme, excepting the group/series separator being an underbar ('_') character instead of a dash character.

We are fine naming operations twice (once per series, per scheme), but we're running into trouble with our recurring maintenance script that invokes 'delete': we're finding that this change violates the verb's expectation of indices' major-, lexicographic-ordering quality. For 'delete' with '--disk-space' defined, the order in which the indices are considered is crucial to operation: it determines the order of accumulation and, eventually, which indices will be deleted.

Consider the following sample that, I think, demonstrates the [part of the code](https://github.com/elastic/curator/blob/v3.2.2/curator/cli/index_selection.py#L109) that we're interested in:

```
	>>> sorted( [
		'common_apache-2000.01.02',
		'common_apache-2000.01.03',
		'common_apache-2000.01.04',
		'common-apache-2000.01.05v1',
		'common-apache-2000.01.06v1'
	] )
	
	[
		'common-apache-2000.01.05v1',
		'common-apache-2000.01.06v1',
		'common_apache-2000.01.02',
		'common_apache-2000.01.03',
		'common_apache-2000.01.04'
	]
```

Here, we want to treat both indices separated by an underbar and indices separated by a dash as being part of the same series: when the newest dashed names grow to a certain size, begin deleting the oldest underbar names. However, the example (together with the code linked) should indicate that, while we can select the right subset of indices, we cannot have them considered in the correct order.

Certainly, we're responsible for our own naming and I wouldn't be surprised to learn that this is a rare case, but there are two specific details that have me considering how difficult it would be to accomodate within the script:

1. We have fully aliased, one-to-one, each old-scheme name to its corresponding new-scheme name, and in both directions. With those aliases, Kibana seems to respect this mapping and can navigate the indices between schemes without issue. If the selection criteria pertaining to names could be expanded to match an index with an alias of the same shape, then we could select for just one scheme, be returned all indices we're concerned about and their names would have the above-named ordering quality.

2. We do specify the timestring for each invocation. It looks as though that that option is currently used only to match out index names that have the right number and placement of numerals, but it should also be possible to infer from it the time unit it corresponds to and order results by that instead.

I'm not too familiar with the specifics of many-to-many aliasing in Elasticsearch and haven't spent too much time thinking about how other verbs would be affected, but thought that I should at least articulate the problem and see how the maintainers feel about it.

issue_title: Heat reporting DELETE_FAILED leads to immediate abort in attempt to delete stack
issue_body:
In `launch_stack` as invoked by a user-induced stack reset, we do this:

```python
    def launch_stack(self,
                     configuration,
                     heat,
                     stack_name,
                     stack_template,
                     reset):
        # [...]
        status = stack.stack_status
        logger.debug("Got [%s] status for [%s]." % (status, stack_name))

        # If stack is undergoing a change of state, wait until it finishes.
        retry = 0
        while 'IN_PROGRESS' in status:
        # [...]

        # If this is a reset request and we didn't just create it, delete the
        # stack and recreate it.
        if reset and not new_stack:
            # Sleep to avoid throttling.
            time.sleep(sleep)

            logger.info("Resetting stack [%s]." % stack_name)
            heat.stacks.delete(stack_id=stack.id)
            status = 'DELETE_IN_PROGRESS'

            # Sleep to avoid throttling.
            time.sleep(sleep)

            # [...]

            # Wait until delete finishes.
            retry = 0
            while ('FAILED' not in status and
                   status != 'DELETE_COMPLETE'):
                if retry:
                    logger.debug("Stack [%s] not ready, with status [%s]. "
                                 "Waiting %s seconds "
                                 "until retry." % (stack_name,
                                                   status,
                                                   sleep))
                    time.sleep(sleep)

                try:
                    logger.debug("Getting stack info for [%s], "
                                 "with previous status [%s]." % (stack_name,
                                                                 status))
                    stack = heat.stacks.get(stack_id=stack.id)
                except HTTPNotFound:
                    status = 'DELETE_COMPLETE'
                else:
                    status = stack.stack_status
                    logger.debug("Got [%s] status "
                                 "for [%s]." % (status, stack_name))
                    retry += 1
                    if retry >= retries:
                        logger.error("Stack [%s], status [%s], "
                                     "took too long to delete.  Giving up "
                                     "after %s retries" % (stack_name,
                                                           status,
                                                           retry))
                        status = 'DELETE_FAILED'

            if status == 'DELETE_COMPLETE':
                logger.info("Stack [%s] deleted successfully.  "
                            "Recreating it." % stack_name)
                res = heat.stacks.create(stack_name=stack_name,
                                         template=stack_template)
        # [...]
        # If stack is suspended, resume it.
        if status == 'SUSPEND_COMPLETE':
            # Sleep to avoid throttling.
            time.sleep(sleep)
        # [...]
        if status not in UP_STATES:
            error_msg = ("Stack [%s] launch "
                         "failed with status [%s]" % (stack_name, status))
            logger.error(error_msg)

```

In other words, if _Heat itself_ reports a deletion failure (setting the stack status to `DELETE_FAILED`), then we're not retrying the delete at all. That means we're being a lot less persistent than we should be: the deletion might be running into a timeout, or a transient failure, and we absolutely should retry it.

What makes this worse is that `launch_stack()`, as I understand it, doesn't do any recovery at all if it encounters a stack already in the `DELETE_FAILED` state, since it skips the `while ('FAILED' not in status and status != 'DELETE_COMPLETE')` loop entirely.

So this means that any time a stack goes to `DELETE_FAILED` as reported by Heat, there's no way for the learner to recover their environment without administrator intervention.

* * *

I *think* we could just replace

```python
            while ('FAILED' not in status and
                   status != 'DELETE_COMPLETE'):
```

with

```python
            while (status != 'DELETE_COMPLETE'):
```

..., relying on 

```python
                    if retry >= retries:
                        logger.error("Stack [%s], status [%s], "
                                     "took too long to delete.  Giving up "
                                     "after %s retries" % (stack_name,
                                                           status,
                                                           retry))
                        status = 'DELETE_FAILED'
```

... to bail us out if even multiple deletion attempts failed.

Thoughts, @arbrandes?

issue_title: Use tag to set stop_on_error for CodeCell (Enhancement)
Node De-Duplication
issue_body:
Npm v3+ installs are maximally flat by default, but versions before that require `npm dedupe` to ensure that two versions of the same package with the same version are not installed.  This is a problem when a module stores module-private data structures that are meant to be shared and loaded only once.

I propose that the version of npm be checked on install, and if it is 3+, install with no modification.  Otherwise, attempt to run `npm dedupe` after `npm install`.  If that fails (as has been seen due to file permissions [issues](https://github.com/jupyter/atom-notebook/issues/32#issuecomment-184922403)), prompt the user to install npm 3+.  

I can make the necessary changes to `setupbase.py`.

seb

issue_title: Fix hotgym_regression_test.py to make it work with nupic.core PR 1236
Way to increase(/decrease) forgetting/synaptic decay at different distances
issue_body:
Improve the way we can hint forgetting of sequences. Or setting contextual closeness. 

The current means to do so are: 

* **maxSequenceLength** 
will restart the sequence learning once the length is reached (~1000)
* **globalDecay**
synapses disintegrate/leak with time. (off, 0)
* **pamLen** 
pay-attention-mode. End of last seq. is connected to the start of the new (correct?) (def. ~3)
* **manual reset** 
by the `r` stream, or `model.sequenceReset()` 

Example use-case:

An easy example is context of words (how semantic vector is constructed in NLP): 
there's a sliding window grouping words that are learned together,
`A quick [brown fox jumps] over a ...`
`[]` define the strongest contextual bond (may, or may not be correct; but thus it's done by the method - using distance). We'd like to extend it to sentences, paragraphs, pages, same document. ... 

Similar example can be done for ECG, or many other data. 

Proposal: 

**Finer control over forgetting**
* maybe a `forgetALittle()` 
would be enough, called after each [triplet], end of sequence/paragraph/... 
* or some defined value/percentage `decaySynapsesBy(x)` 
but that can be tricky how it accumulates on multiple calls. 

Usage:

A task specific model/code calls it at multiple places.


CC @subutai @rhyolight

issue_title: Test H10 in TM integration test is invalid
issue_body:
There is an error in the loop on the second trial which caused the output to have the desired stats. The test fails when the loop is corrected. 

Secondly, the setting of "predictedSegmentDecrement" to 0.004 has no effect when the loop is corrected - indicating that there may need to be some algorithm enhancement in order to make the parameter have the desired effect. (Other than lowering the permanence of the Synapse resulting in eventual removal of the Synapse - which works).

Line 532: extensive_temporal_memory_test.py
``` Python

for _ in xrange(10):
      self.feedTM(sequenceNoisy[i])
```

**Should be:**

``` Python
for i in xrange(10):
      self.feedTM(sequenceNoisy[i])
```

Where the underscore ("_") should be an "i" so that all the sequences are actually entered.

This is the test description:

> Orphan Decay mechanism reduce predicted inactive cells (extra predictions).
    Test feeds in noisy sequences (X = 0.05) to TM with and without orphan decay.
    TM with orphan decay should has many fewer predicted inactive columns.
    Parameters the same as B11, and sequences like H9.

When the loop is "corrected" you will see by setting the "predictedSegmentDecrement" variable to "0.000" that it has no effect on the "predictedInactiveColumns" mean or sum. I had to set it to "0.999" in order for their to be noticeable reduction (by only half) - which I think may need some care and loving in order to have a more pronounced effect.

issue_title: support int16 grayscale images
issue_body:
This is often the case with medical (MRI) data.

Required changes would be in [ToTensor](https://github.com/pytorch/vision/blob/master/torchvision/transforms.py#L43-L49) probably something like:
```python
# PIL image mode: 1, L, P, I, F, RGB, YCbCr, RGBA, CMYK
if pic.mode == 'YCbCr':
  nchannel = 3
else:
  nchannel = len(pic.mode)
# handle PIL Image
buf = pic.tobytes()
if len(buf) > pic.width * pic.height * nchannel:
  img = torch.LongTensor(torch.LongStorage.from_buffer(buf))
else:
  img = torch.ByteTensor(torch.ByteStorage.from_buffer(pic.tobytes()))
img = img.view(pic.size[1], pic.size[0], nchannel)
```

as well as in [ToPILImage](https://github.com/pytorch/vision/blob/master/torchvision/transforms.py#L66-L68) (just remove normalization to [0, 255] here?).

However I can't assess possible side effects. `int16` support may be not very good in pillow (e.g. `plt.imshow(Image.fromarray(int16_np_array))` does not work) also there may be other transforms which depend on [0, 255] byte range.

issue_title: account.billing_info raises AttributeError on new accounts.
issue_body:
Repeat of #98, but I'm having this occur in 2.2.17. I should note I'm on python 3.5.0, just in case.

Using the example in the docs for creating a new account, and then a new billing info object:

```py
account = Account(account_code='1')
account.email = 'verena@example.com'
account.first_name = 'Verena'
account.last_name = 'Example'
account.save()

# account.billing_info here raises AttributeError, 
# so I copy-paste the example code exactly, including the get.

account = Account.get('1')
billing_info = account.billing_info #  but this still raises an AttributeError!
billing_info.token_id = 'TOKEN_ID'
billing_info.save()
```

I seem to have been able to resolve this by calling `account.update_billing_info` with a new BillingInfo object I create and add the token to, like so:

```py
account = Account.get('1')
billing_info = BillingInfo()
billing_info.token_id = 'TOKEN_ID'
account.update_billing_info(billing_info)
```

Before I arrived to the solution above, I also tried attaching the billing info object I created to the account and saving it, but that raised an unrelated AttributeError, so I didn't continue down that path. 

While I have a solution that works for me as-is, I'm curious as to whether this is intended to be used this way, or if I'm going against the grain, since the example differed from my implementation. 

Thanks for your time!

issue_title: S3 Feature Addition - will break current usage of list and listdir
issue_body:
### This feature addition will break current use of `S3Client.list()` and `S3Client.listdir()`.

My question is... **_Since this is a niche-case feature that will break people's existing code who use one of these functions, would it still be valuable to submit a PR for this feature addition?_**

Due to a specific use-case where I need to know the total number and size of files copied between s3 buckets, I have modified `S3Client.listdir()` and `S3Client.list()` such that they return a generator consisting of `(path/fn, s3_item)`. The inclusion of this modification allows one to get [metadata about each file](http://boto.cloudhackers.com/en/latest/ref/s3.html#module-boto.s3.key) listed.

Current use of `list` or `listdir` looks like the following:

```python
from luigi import s3
s3_client = s3.S3Client()
for item in s3_client.list('s3://path/to/dir'):
    print(item)  #prints filename
for item in s3_client.listdir('s3://path/to/dir'):
    print(item)  #prints filename with path
```

If a PR for this feature is submitted and approved, usage would be the following:

```python
from luigi import s3
s3_client = s3.S3Client()
for path, item in s3_client.list('s3://path/to/dir'):
    print(path)  #prints filename
    print(item.last_modified)
    print(item.size)
for path, item in s3_client.listdir('s3://path/to/dir'):
    print(path)  #prints filename with path
    print(item.last_modified)
    print(item.size)
```

issue_title: build_hdf5_image_dataset way too slow (and some questions)
issue_body:
Hi!

As reported in https://github.com/tflearn/tflearn/issues/303 the build_hdf5_image_dataset is VERY slow. I tried to build a hdf5 on 60K images for almost 3 days now! I didn't try using ```image_preloader``` (or at least I closed it after 10-15 minutes while the training didn't start).

1) Is somebody investigating this issue? If not well, I would throw in some hours
2) My "top" suspect is line [408](https://github.com/cristi-zz/tflearn/blob/master/tflearn/data_utils.py#L408) in ```build_hdf5_image_dataset```. The 2'nd suspect is PIL. Why? I am using hdf5 format for a while but always wrote big chunks of data at a time (and the writting goes ~at I/O speed). The file list is built automatically because I have to filter out the "garbage": partially downloaded files, RGBA files, etc. I do this buy opening each file using scikit-image and checking for exceptions, no of channels, etc. Reading 60K images and writting a csv out of them is fast (~minutes) But only the profiling will tell for sure.
3) (somehow unrelated) ```image_preloader``` does the same thing slightly different. Wouldn't it be best that the two methods have the same interface (and of course mostly the same code)?

I have some questions:

1) How hard is to add a new dependency to tflearn? (scikit-image for example)
2) What library do you favour for multithreading? (again, to speed up hdf5 buildup)
3) There are no tests for data_utils.py? 

Thanks!
Cristi

issue_title: Incorrect Mimetype/Content-Type Handling
issue_body:
I think there may be a bug in Content-Type/Mimetype handling.

If an operation has a single `produces` type defined in swagger and the implementation returns a flask Response object, both the swagger `produces` value and the Response object's `content-type` are returned. Example:

```yaml
operationId: app.get_greeting
produces:
  - text/plain
```

with the implementation:

```python
def post_greeting(name: str) -> str:
    response = flask.make_response('Hello {}\n'.format(name))
    response.headers['content-type'] = 'text/plain'
    response.mimetype = 'text/plain'
    return response
```

`curl -v` request to the route returns:

```
< Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8
< Content-Length: 11
< Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8
< Content-Length: 11
```

If you don't specifically set the Response object's content-type or mimetype, then the following is returned. 

```
< Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8
< Content-Length: 11
< Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8
< Content-Length: 11
```

This is a bigger issue when an operation has multiple `produces` values. If you use the same code as above with an added `application/json` to the `produces` values, `application/json` is always returned in addition to `text/plain`. 

```
< Content-Type: application/json
< Content-Length: 11
< Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8
< Content-Length: 11
```

I suspect that the code causing this issue is in `connexion/decorator.py::BeginOfRequestLifecycleDecorator.get_response_container`.

issue_title: Proposal for event handling refactoring..
issue_body:
As of 0.9.1.0 we do support various combinations for adding event handlers, some of them seem overdone and unnecessary. They add to a code complexity and don't bring anything extra to the feature-set. Below I discuss what I think are the required feature-set for EventHandling. Asking the community for comments.

* Watch methods
    * watch an Mo for any change. Provide a callback function that will be called anytime any property of a specified Mo changes.
    * watch an Mo for a specific condition `mo.prop == value`; where value can also be a single value or a list of values. This is a derivative of the above and ideally can be done at the application layer by the user, but this seems to be the most common case a usage scenario and so we should support this in the base sdk.

* Other considerations:
    * We also need to support both the `push` and `poll` mode for the above combinations.
      * Push mode - SDK continuously listens to the events from the server and acts when the correct event is found
      * Poll mode - SDK polls the servers regularly for the required Mo and checks the condition

* Wait method"
    * Additionally, we should also have a wait method that can keep the script alive for the duration of the eventHandler.

issue_title: Online error reporting
issue_body:
The DukeDS team has asked for error reports from DukeDSClient, when operations fail. Some of these errors would correspond to API server-side errors, and it's useful to have that data. Other errors occur between DukeDSClient and the object storage or network, and these would not appear in the API server logs.

Since the client logs errors to the terminal, client-side errors are effectively lost, unless the user passes them along. As such, we don't have a good idea of error frequency or type.

We should investigate using an error reporting library or service in DukeDSClient, informing users of the reporting and allowing them to opt-out.

issue_title: Evaluation of BV multiplication overflow/underflow operators not working
issue_body:
When handed to the evaluation on a model, expressions containing operators for checking for (un)signed over/underflow of bitvector multiplication are not evaluated to a boolean constant as one would expect. Instead, just its arguments are evaluated.

These operators are:
```
bvsmul_noovfl
bvumul_noovfl
bvsmul_noudfl
```

The problem can be reproduced e.g. with the following SMT 2 specification (but also occurs for similar expressions constructed and evaluated using C/C++ bindings):
```
(declare-const a (_ BitVec 8))
(declare-const b (_ BitVec 8))
(declare-const c (_ BitVec 8))
(assert (and (= a #x04) (= b #x05) (= c #x74)))
(check-sat)
(get-model)
(eval (bvsmul_noovfl a b)) ; should be true
(eval (bvsmul_noovfl c b)) ; should be false
(eval (bvumul_noovfl a b)) ; should be true
(eval (bvsmul_noudfl a b)) ; should be true
```

issue_title: Can not run a subprocess in a test: "the child watcher does not have a loop attached"
issue_body:
## Long story short

The loop created for the testing purpose doesn't allow you to spawn subprocesses using `asyncio.create_subprocess_*`.

## Expected behaviour

You can spawn subprocesses without problem like you would do with a default loop.

## Actual behaviour

When you try to spawn a subprocess, you automatically get:

```
self = <asyncio.unix_events.SafeChildWatcher object at 0x7fe80eb15e10>, pid = 9399
callback = <bound method _UnixSelectorEventLoop._child_watcher_callback of <_UnixSelectorEventLoop running=False closed=False debug=False>>
args = (<_UnixSubprocessTransport pid=9399 running>,)

    def add_child_handler(self, pid, callback, *args):
        if self._loop is None:
            raise RuntimeError(
>               "Cannot add child handler, "
                "the child watcher does not have a loop attached")
E           RuntimeError: Cannot add child handler, the child watcher does not have a loop attached

/usr/lib64/python3.6/asyncio/unix_events.py:858: RuntimeError
```

## Steps to reproduce

```python
import asyncio


# tokio event loop does not allow to override attributes
def skip_if_no_dict(loop):
    if not hasattr(loop, '__dict__'):
        pytest.skip("can not override loop attributes")


def stopper(loop):
    def f(*args):
        loop.call_later(0.001, loop.stop)
    return f


async def test_subprocess_co(loop):
    proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_shell("exit 0", loop=loop)
    await proc.wait()
    assert proc.retcode == 0


def test_subprocess(loop, mocker):
    skip_if_no_dict(loop)
    loop.run_until_complete(test_subprocess_co(loop))
```

issue_title: Client max size ignored on cloning web request object
issue_body:
## Long story short

`_client_max_size` private attr not specified on cloning `web_request.BaseRequest` object.

## Expected behaviour

When `web.Application` using custom `client_max_size` and in same time some middleware that cloned request, like [https_middleware](http://aiohttp-middlewares.readthedocs.io/en/latest/#aiohttp-https), there is **still able** to upload any file larger then 1Mb as default `Request._client_max_size` will be overwritten with value from `Application`.

## Actual behaviour

When `web.Application` using custom `client_max_size` and in same time some middleware that cloned request, like [https_middleware](http://aiohttp-middlewares.readthedocs.io/en/latest/#aiohttp-https), there is **unable** to upload any file larger then 1Mb as default `Request._client_max_size` not overwritten with value from `Application` after `request.clone` call.

## Steps to reproduce

- Enable [https_middleware](http://aiohttp-middlewares.readthedocs.io/en/latest/#aiohttp-https) or any other middleware which clone `request` before passing to inner handler
- Run aiohttp under nginx with HTTPS support
- Upload any file larger then 1Mb
- Get `ValueError` on `await request.post()` or `HTTPRequestEntityTooLarge` on `await request.read()`

## Your environment

- `Python==3.6.3`
- `aiohttp==2.3.1`

I'm trying to improve performance of the parser on a fairly messy list containing individuals, households, and corporations. For individuals and households the parser works great. 
For corporations I see lots of listings like:

Currently the tagging for that will be:
```
| ACME | CorporationName             |

| A         | CorporationName             |

I think ideally the result would be something like:

| ACME | CorporationName             |

issue_title: SMC (VMC) get_validators_max_index
issue_body:
### What is wrong?
If the collator deposit (register) on SMC, `get_validators_max_index` will return a different result, and then the `get_eligible_proposer` (`get_eligible_collator`) function will return a different result with the same input for different lastest block number.

### How can it be fixed
1. When we were using "shuffling cycle" in the old PoC, we set `validators[validator_index].cycle` as the cycle that the validator would be included after. Now we can set `collators[validator_index].period` as the period number that the collator would be included after. 
2. Any better solution for `get_validators_max_index`....? The current solution limits the "maximum collator size" to `1024` so it's not cool.

issue_title: Use simple coder class instantiation instead of on-the-fly subclassing for particular types?
issue_body:
### What was wrong?

Coder classes currently use programmatic subclassing via the built-in `type` function as an intermediate step toward creating a callable which performs the actual encoding/decoding of values:

https://github.com/ethereum/eth-abi/blob/master/eth_abi/encoding.py#L62
https://github.com/ethereum/eth-abi/blob/master/eth_abi/decoding.py#L41

### How can it be fixed?

Would it be simpler to just pass in coder settings as keyword arguments to an `__init__` method on coder classes?  So something like this:

```python
class BaseEncoder(BaseCoder):
    def __init__(self, **kwargs):
        cls = type(self)
        
        for key, value in kwargs.items():
            if not hasattr(cls, key):
                raise AttributeError(
                    'Property {key} not found on {cls_name} class. '
                    '`{cls_name}.__init__` only accepts keyword arguments which are '
                    'present on the {cls_name} class.'.format(
                        key=repr(key),
                        cls_name=cls.__name__,
                    )
                )
            
            setattr(self, key, value)

        self.validate()

    ...
```

### Disclaimer

If there's a particular reason that this approach was chosen, then just ignore me 😄 .

issue_title: Add Google App Engine support to py3 fork
django-allauth compatibility
issue_body:
For me `django-axes` didn't work with `django-allauth` package by default.

## Problem 1:

After some digging in code I've made my own `apps.py` that monkey patches allauth's `LoginView` the same way as axes does it to django's `LoginView`:
```
class AppConfig(apps.AppConfig):
    name = 'my_app_name'

    def ready(self):
        from allauth.account.views import LoginView  # <-- Just this line is changed
        from django.utils.decorators import method_decorator

        from axes import signals    # we must load signals
        from axes.decorators import axes_dispatch
        from axes.decorators import axes_form_invalid

        LoginView.dispatch = method_decorator(axes_dispatch)(LoginView.dispatch)
        LoginView.form_invalid = method_decorator(axes_form_invalid)(LoginView.form_invalid)
```

It may be useful to either point that out in readme or to modify `apps.py` to try to detect allauth and patch it too.

## Problem 2:
axes rely on having login id stored under `AXES_USERNAME_FORM_FIELD` key both in request.POST and in `credentials` arg of `user_login_failed` signal. This is not the case with allauth. They allways use `login` key in post POST data but it becomes `username` key in `credentials` dict in signal handler.

When I put `AXES_USERNAME_FORM_FIELD='login'` in use with `AXES_ONLY_USER_FAILURES=True` it causes empty username go to cache key and therefore locking everybody out when anyone is locked. They all get the same cache key then.

I'm using:
django-axes==4.0.1
django-allauth==0.34.0 (note: 0.31.0 didn't work with my fix either, had to upgrade)
django==1.11

issue_title: pyls incorrectly requires that `textDocument/didOpen` be called before fufilling any requests
issue_body:
See this issue in the official LSP repo: https://github.com/Microsoft/language-server-protocol/issues/177

Per [@debaumer's comment](https://github.com/Microsoft/language-server-protocol/issues/177#issuecomment-278299653), language servers should be able to fulfill requests regardless of whether or not the text document is open. 

For example, `pyls` will raise a `KeyError` if you call `hover` on a document that has not been opened yet. According to the above, this seems to be incorrect behavior - pyls should be able to read this document from disk and fulfill the hover request.

issue_title: Isolate server dependencies in VM/container image or similar
issue_body:
In order to run the CalDAV/CardDAV tests against ownCloud, one currently has to manually install the PHP-dependencies ownCloud requries. This requires root privileges on the current box, which is not acceptable for Travis, given that they're about to phase out exactly that (see #389).

I'm imagining something like this:

* Create vagrant (or docker?) image that contains preinstalled ownCloud which autostarts on boot.
* Port forward ownCloud's listening port to host OS.
* Run tests on host OS.

What we choose (and which OS we boot) primarily depends on what has the shortest boot times, because the tests are slow as-is.

issue_title: bin/upgrade script authentication handshake
issue_body:
When using the `bin/upgrade` command authentication is required for some commands (e.g. `list` and `install`).
Currently the authentication information can be provided by using `--auth` or by setting the environment variable.
This results in having an administrator password in the bash history or somewhere on the machine, which is not very nice. It also requires to have an administrator user with known password (we want to get rid of this for privacy reasons).

The script should be able to make a handshake with a running Plone instance and automatically authenticate (e.g. as app or site owner).
I think verifying that the client script (`bin/upgrade`) can read private files from the filesystem where the server runs we have enough security (meaning: the user has access to the server and could make a `bin/instance makeuser` anyway and would have full access).
Therefore we could do this:
- `bin/upgrade` requests an authentication session from the Zope instance
- The Zope instance writes a private file to the filesystem, containing a hash
- `bin/upgrade` reads the file and sends the hash in subsequent requests
- The Zope instance automatically authenticates as Manager user
- The file is removed when the session is ended.

issue_title: Custom firefox profile doesn't work in Windows
issue_body:
[Documentation](http://splinter.readthedocs.org/en/latest/drivers/firefox.html#how-to-use-a-specific-profile-for-firefox) says you can select an existing firefox profile instead of creating a temporary one each time:

    browser = Browser('firefox', profile='my_profile')

But this doesn't actually work, it doesn't know where to search for the profile:

```
browser = Browser('firefox', profile='default')
...
WindowsError: [Error 3] The system cannot find the path specified: 'default/*.*'
```

same for `profile='272kp1a1.default'`, `profile='272kp1a1'`, etc. though it isn't explained in the docs which type of string is correct.

issue_title: With 'json_verbose', the Writer does caching for dict keys
issue_body:
~~~python
from transit.writer import Writer
from StringIO import StringIO
io = StringIO()
writer = Writer(io, "json_verbose")
writer.write([
    {'myKey1': 42},
    {'myKey1': 42},
])
print str(io.getvalue())
~~~

Expected output:  **(I confirmed this is what Clojure does)**

~~~
[{"myKey1":42},{"myKey1":42}]
~~~

Actual output:

~~~
[{"myKey1":42},{"^0":42}]
~~~

It's in a weird partly-caching mode.

My ClojureScript expects `:json-verbose`, so this is causing bad decode issues. And I can't switch to `:json` because of https://github.com/cognitect/transit-cljs/issues/22. Hopefully I'm looking at this right?

issue_title: Setting transport class in logging dict config
issue_body:
Hello, I have encountered a problem after upgrading from raven 5.x and not sure how to deal with it better.

In older raven versions it was allowed to set Transport in DSN parameter. Since my applications make use of logging dict config, I used to set Transport there. From now on it produces a warning `Transport selection via DSN is deprecated`. I was not able to set Transport class explicitly in logging dict config:

```
handlers:
  default:
    level: DEBUG
    class: logging.StreamHandler
    formatter: default
    stream: ext://sys.stdout
  sentry:
    level: INFO
    class: raven.handlers.logging.SentryHandler
    dsn: http://*****:*****@sentry.*****.local/11
    transport: raven.transport.threaded_requests.ThreadedRequestsHTTPTransport
    environment: development
```

Judging by the code, SentryHandler won't be instantiated if `transport` is of type `str` and not `callable`.
I wonder if there is an other way to set Transport class in logging dict config? Might an extra parameter `transport_class` be implemented in next versions so as to instruct SentryHandler to load the desired type of Transport by its string identifier?

issue_title: Support for teardown
issue_body:
Hi Sentry team,

Is there any way to teardown/uninstall Sentry?

I've got an application embedded in a long-running process, which is installed and uninstalled on-demand. 

```python
import myapp
myapp.install()
# .. some time later
myapp.unistall()
```

The `uninstall` reverses the `install`, such as removing UI elements, disconnecting from event handlers/listeners and clearing memory.

The application is using Raven for communication with Sentry, but I'm struggling to find some way of reliably undoing or otherwise uninstalling Raven once installed. Primarily the added `sys.excepthook` and log handlers, and likely other things I am unaware is being installed.

At the moment, the `Client()` and `setup_logging` is being called repeatedly, which is registering new excepthooks and log handlers over and over.

Here is what I use to install Sentry.

```python
import logging
from raven import Client
from raven.handlers.logging import SentryHandler
from raven.conf import setup_logging

client = Client(MY_DSN)

handler = SentryHandler(client)
handler.setLevel(logging.ERROR)

setup_logging(handler)
```

What would be an equivalent "uninstall"?

Thanks,
Marcus

issue_title: Support Django's sensitive data decorators
issue_body:
Django has some decorators that allow developers to annotate functions and methods with information about sensitive variables and post data: https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/2.0/howto/error-reporting/#filtering-error-reports

As far as I can tell the Django client from raven doesn't do anything with this information.

Processors are an alternative solution, but don't support granular control of when to filter something. i.e. key can be a safe var in one function, but highly sensitive in another one.

Is it possible to take these decorators into account? Would it be possible to write a filter that does so? Is it possible to somehow reuse the filter logic in [SafeExceptionReporterFilter](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/2.0/howto/error-reporting/#custom-error-reports) 

I found an old PR (#345) that tries to support sensitive_post_params, but it got abandoned.

I'm willing to work on this, but I'm not familiar with the inner workings of Raven so hopefully someone can provide some guidance before I put some actual effort into this.

issue_title: 改善Notebook渲染效率
issue_body:
## 问题

目前在渲染一个图表的过程中， `_repr_html__` 总共调用三次 `engine.render` 函数，也就是会创建三个同样的 `EchartsEnvironment` 实例，而 `EchartsEnvironment` 的创建需要花费更多的时间，这里可以合并。

另一方面，同一个 Cell 的模板被分成了 notebook.html / notebook_chart_component.html / notebook_dom.html 等三个文件，可读性较差，可以整合。

## 改进

- 整合三个模板文件，移除 notebook_chart_component.html / notebook_dom.html  等两个文件
- 整合模板变量字典
- 移除 `_render_notebook_component_` 和 `_render_notebook_dom_` 两个方法

## 备注

- 本次改善为内部实现优化，不会改变既有的公有 API
- 该特性预计在 v0.3.4 / v0.4.0 加入

issue_title: add Extractions endpoint to ASM policy
During functional testing in 11.5.4, requests_params with filters fail
issue_body:
The test test/functional/test_requests_params.py::test_get_dollar_filtered_collection fails in the following way against an 11.5.4 device:

```iControlUnexpectedHTTPError: 400 Unexpected Error: Bad Request for uri: https://10.190.3.58:443/mgmt/tm/ltm/pool/?%24filter=partition+eq+za
Text: u'{"code":400,"message":"Query parameter %24filter is invalid.","errorStack":[]}'```

After manually testing, we've concluded that this occurs due to the way the 11.5.4 device fails to interpret the encoded dollar sign (%24). We can work around this by preventing requests from encoding our requests parameters. One can find an example of this fix here: https://devcentral.f5.com/questions/icontrol-rest-filter-processing-not-http-compliant

issue_title: Raise an exception if no SQLAlchemy session was set
issue_body:
I just inherited from the wrong class, so no `session` attribute was set. Instead of a clear excetption, the code failed implicitly when the object was tried to be added to the session.

It would be a nice convenience feature to raise a ValueError or similiar. 

Proof of concept:

``` python
from factory.alchemy import SQLAlchemyModelFactory as Factory
from factory.fuzzy import FuzzyText
from flask.ext.sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy
from sqlalchemy import (Column, String)

db = SQLAlchemy()


class Traffic(db.Model):
    traffic_id = Column(String(15), nullable=False, index=True, primary_key=True)


class TrafficFactory(Factory):
    class Meta:
        model = Traffic
        # this was missing:
        # session = db.session

    traffic_id = FuzzyText()


if __name__ == '__main__':
    assert TrafficFactory()

```

Returns: 

```
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'add'
```

The place where it fails is alchemy.py:46.
Adding  `if not session: raise ValueError("No session provided")` before that line should be sufficient.

issue_title: @log_capture() doesn't play nice with other decorators
issue_body:
Considering the following:


```
class MyTestTestCase(unittest.TestCase):

    @log_capture()
    def test_log_capture_only(self, log_capture):
        self.assertIsInstance(log_capture, LogCaptureForDecorator)

    @log_capture()
    @patch('apps.wideband.manager.order.OrderManager')
    def test_patch_and_log(self, mock, log_capture):
        self.assertIsInstance(log_capture, LogCaptureForDecorator)
        self.assertIsInstance(mock, MagicMock)

    @patch('apps.wideband.manager.order.OrderManager')
    @log_capture()
    def test_log_then_patch(self, log_capture, mock):
        self.assertIsInstance(log_capture, LogCaptureForDecorator)
        self.assertIsInstance(mock, MagicMock)
```
The following error is generated:

```
Error
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/testfixtures/utils.py", line 42, in wrapping
    return wrapped(*args, **kw)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/mock/mock.py", line 1305, in patched
    return func(*args, **keywargs)
TypeError: test_patch_and_log() takes exactly 3 arguments (2 given)

Failure
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/mock/mock.py", line 1305, in patched
    return func(*args, **keywargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/testfixtures/utils.py", line 42, in wrapping
    return wrapped(*args, **kw)
  File "/opt/source/apps/wideband/tests/managers/test_order.py", line 106, in test_log_then_patch
    self.assertIsInstance(log_capture, LogCaptureForDecorator)
AssertionError: <MagicMock name='OrderManager' id='139896440938384'> is not an instance of <class 'testfixtures.logcapture.LogCaptureForDecorator'>)
```

This particular test case was tacked on to the end of a WIP file, so the line numbers won't quite match up.

The first test is fine.
The second test is not populating an argument for `log_capture`.
The third test actually has the order of the types swapped around.

When doing tests that have multiple patches, functionality has been that the `@patch` nearest the `def` appears first in the argument list. I would expect that the same applies here.

Using 
* Python 2.7
* Django 1.10.5
* mock 2.0.0
* testfixtures 5.4.0

issue_title: yara-python 3.5.0 on PyPI has stale .y and .l generated code files
issue_body:
FYI you likely want to have sdist handle updating the .y and .l generated code files

The yara-python 3.5.0 version provides via https://pypi.python.org/pypi/yara-python/3.5.0 contains stale .y and .l generated code files e.g. yara/libyara/grammar.c still includes stdint.h while gammar.y references yara/integers.h. Currently this causes yara-python unable to build on Windows.

issue_title: AWSTemplateFormatVersion: 2010-09-09 results in JSON serializable error
issue_body:
When a date is used in yaml, it results in an JSON serialization error.
Is this a bug in cfn-sphere or in the yaml file ?

See here:

Description: Example for BugReport

Resources:

    chunks = self.iterencode(o, _one_shot=True)

issue_title: Add ReportBuildStatus Parameter to CodeBuild Source
issue_body:
It looks like there's an additional parameter for the codebuild Source object that is missing

`AttributeError: Source object does not support attribute ReportBuildStatus`

AWS Reference:  https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-properties-codebuild-project-source.html#cfn-codebuild-project-source-reportbuildstatus

issue_title: Template.add_resource should only allow resources
issue_body:
`Template.add_resource` really should only allow an object of AWSObject to be added - right now this is caught by this line of code https://github.com/cloudtools/troposphere/blob/master/troposphere/__init__.py#L565

but not well - and it's mostly a side effect.  It results in a hard to understand exception as well:

`AttributeError: 'dict' object has no attribute 'title'`

This all came up because a new user of stacker was trying to call `add_resource` in a blueprint on an `AWSProperty` instance.  I figure there's some room here for better error handling.

I started a PR on this, but ran into issues because `cloudformation.AWSCustomObject` is not a subclass of `AWSObject`.  Changing it to a subclass of `AWSObject` resulted in this test failing since it loads every class that is a subclass of `AWSObject`: https://github.com/cloudtools/troposphere/blob/master/tests/test_int_type.py

I'll probably try to dive back into this later, but wanted to open this in case anyone else has any ideas on what the right way to fix this is.

issue_title: PublishOptions broken, exclude_me not acknowledged
issue_body:
Tests running on Python 3.5.1, autobahn 0.13.0, crossbar 0.13.0, using asyncio version.

Simple test case:

```python
class Test(ApplicationSession):
    async def onJoin(self, details):
        logging.info(await self.subscribe(self.foo, 'foo', SubscribeOptions(details_arg='details')))
        logging.info('Subscribed, sending test...')
        logging.info(await self.publish('foo', 42, options=PublishOptions(acknowledge=True, exclude_me=False)))
        logging.info('Test sent.')

    def foo(self, bar: int, details: EventDetails):
        logging.info('foo %s', details)
        self.disconnect()
        asyncio.get_event_loop().stop()

ApplicationRunner('ws://localhost:8080', 'test').run(Test)
```

The event is never triggered, the output is merely:

```
2016-03-29T12:28:02 Subscription(id=4306902726829498, is_active=True)
2016-03-29T12:28:02 Registered, sending test...
2016-03-29T12:28:02 Publication(id=358474513636002, was_encrypted=False)
2016-03-29T12:28:02 Test sent.
```

I was encountering this while playing around with custom serialisers, and looking at the actual serialised message, it's merely:

    [16, 2, {'acknowledge': True}, 'foo', (42,)]

The `exclude_me=False` option is getting lost somewhere. When setting `exclude_me=True` it *does* show up in the serialised message!

Somewhat unrelated but also concerning, when setting `eligible_authrole=['foo']`, the Crossbar router(!) has this in its log:

```
2016-03-29T12:29:08+0200 [Router       2277] Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/autobahn/wamp/websocket.py", line 88, in onMessage
    for msg in self._serializer.unserialize(payload, isBinary):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/autobahn/wamp/serializer.py", line 131, in unserialize
    msg = Klass.parse(raw_msg)

    for _authrole in option_exclude_authrole:
UnboundLocalError: local variable 'option_exclude_authrole' referenced before assignment
```

Here is a MWE to reproduce the problem:

issue_title: Timezones are ignored when converting datetimes
issue_body:
The [API doc](https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/directions/intro) specifies that departure/arrival times are in seconds since January 1, 1970 UTC. When generating the appropriate timestamp  from a Python `datetime` object in  [`convert.time`](https://github.com/googlemaps/google-maps-services-python/blob/master/googlemaps/convert.py#L188) the library ignores attached timezones, assuming local `datetime`s.

For example the following code returns wrong traffic information:
```
    gmaps = googlemaps.Client(key="XXX", client_id="XXX", client_secret="XXX")
    times = [
        datetime(2017, 9, 18, 8, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc),
        datetime(2017, 9, 18, 9, 0, tzinfo=timezone(timedelta(hours=1))),
        datetime(2017, 9, 18, 8, 0, tzinfo=timezone(timedelta(hours=1)))
    ]
    for time in times:
        res = gmaps.directions('Montauban', 'Toulouse', departure_time=time)
        print('datetime: {} - duration: {}'.format(time, res[0]['legs'][0]['duration_in_traffic']['text']))
```

, where the first two times correspond to the exact same timestamp and the third time is an hour before the other two, print the following:

```
datetime: 2017-09-18 08:00:00+00:00 - duration: 50 mins
datetime: 2017-09-18 08:00:00+01:00 - duration: 50 mins
datetime: 2017-09-18 09:00:00+01:00 - duration: 55 mins
```

I think that the right fix would be to use the timezone aware `timestamp` datetime method rather than `timetuple` in `convert.time`. Was there a reason for using timetuple?

I can open a PR for that if it helps.

issue_title: MAC layer may retransmit one frame constantly, if it does not receive ACK
issue_body: If radio supports ACK timeout, and radio reports that No ACK received, Mac::TransmitDoneTask would retransmit the same frame constantly.

issue_title: BUG: Failed Commissioning results in "Frame rx failed, error:UnknownNeighbor" on second try
issue_body:
Scenario:
    NCP + Border Router
    Joining Device

During first attempt at commissioning, something goes wrong - commissioning fails.

During the second attempt, the joiner reports:
 [INFO]-MAC-----: Frame rx failed, error:UnknownNeighbor, len:78, seqnum:228, type:Data, src:0x6c00, dst:ffeeb4c865ab8cdf, sec:n

Steps to reproduce:
    Start the commissioning process (attempt 1)
    during the process, at some point before completion cause the join to fail.
    Example: remove power from joining device
    Example: Put either Joiner or NCP into an RF shield box
    Harder : Introduce a very noisy "RF jammer"


    Start second commissioning attempt 

During the second attempt - during the "CoAP-Secure" transfer the Joining device reports:

 [INFO]-MAC-----: Frame rx failed, error:UnknownNeighbor, len:78, seqnum:228, type:Data, src:0x6c00, dst:ffeeb4c865ab8cdf, sec:n

======
ideas to fix
======

At the discovery request - clean up the tables in the NCP (joiner, leader, etc)
This could be a denial of service attack, an attacker could snoop - and send random random discovery requests causing tables to clean when they should not.

Another would be to clean up at the end of a failed joining operation.

Another approach would be to do this only at the Relay Tx/Rx stage
Rational:  The Commissioner is present, and has agreed/authenticated the join request via cryptographic means "this is the device", and is willing to commission this specific device.

Something else??

Suggestions? We don't have a fix yet.

issue_title: DEBUG UART
issue_body:
Observation: often physical devices have more then 1 physical UART

1) The NCP does have support to push the DEBUG LOG upto the host platform over a "reverse channel" 
This is nice but depending upon log volume - the rate of increased traffic causes other problems.

2) In the GRL test harness, it took talks to the CLI and does not expect log messages.
Using a second UART for LOGs will make it easier to debug certain issues.

3) In the POSIX platform, when debugging problems that go wrong in self-test-automation-scripts the LOG feature interferes with and messes up the UNIT test, in the CLI the easiest solution is to provide a CLI command where you can specify a filename to capture the debug log output.

We have this working internal at TI for CC2538 and CC2652 and POSIX platforms and are looking at pushing this up.

issue_title: Compiler vrs Compiler issues, vrs IDE issues.
issue_body:
Open thread relies upon a number of standard C header files that are different across compilers, and compiler releases.

For example:

#include <string.h> - depending on the compiler does not have some functions.
This also varies by compiler version, (ie: a fix was introduce in a later version)

In the GNU Autoconf world this is solved via either  "libiberty' or various "#ifdefs"
GCC supports an   #include_next <filename> scheme - other compilers do not.

a) This does not play nicely with IDEs that do not support Autoconf.
b) This also makes code "messy" with lots of #if/#else/#endif fixes.

I propose the following solution:

If a standard header files needs to be fixed for a specific tool.
Then - the following action is taken.

Step 1:

A "pseudo-replacement" header file is created in a new directory called: "fixed"
    #include <openthread/platform/fixed/fixed_FILENAME.H>

Alternatively it could be placed in   ${openthread}/src/<somewhere>/fixed/fixed_FILENAME.H
The concern here is if thread ever provids a "C++" api - with functions defined within a class.

Note: specifically the original filename should not be used "as is", but a standard prefix or suffix be added to the original filename to make it unique. In this example I propose: "fixed_FILENAME.h"

Example:  #include <stdio.h>
Becomes:     #include <openthread/platform/fixed/stdio.h 

Step 2:

This new <openthread/platform/fixed/fixed_FILENAME.H>
Must eventually include the tool chain provided FILENAME.H

But also contains any required compiler/library fixes in the form of:  #if/#else/#endif

Step 3:  Problematic Thread code then includes the "fixed" header file in lue of the standard header file.

issue_title: 4 space? style?
issue_body:
Hey pex maintainers,

Curious what your opinions on 4 space indents are... I presume the style in this codebase is an extension of twitter's internal guidance? 

We (linkedin) switched over to 4 space (and mostly pep8) early last year and it's been great. The transition has been pretty easy too. Wondering if y'all are open to the idea of moving pex to 4 space indents and perhaps prescribing some additional style guidance for contribution?

If not, feel free to close.

issue_title: Adjust setup.py's install_requires
issue_body:
setup.py limits setuptools in its install_requires to >=2.2,<8.  This breaks the build of pex on Ubuntu 15.04 where we have setuptools 12.2.  It'll break on Debian too after Jessie is released.

I added the following very simple patch, which so far seems to fix the problem (more testing ahead).

```
--- a/setup.py
+++ b/setup.py
@@ -36,7 +36,7 @@
     'pex.bin',
   ],
   install_requires = [
-    'setuptools>=2.2,<8',
+    'setuptools>=2.2',
   ],
   tests_require = [
     'mock',
```

issue_title: Add a 'Using pants for Go' page to the docsite.
issue_body: The plugin should have a README that is linked to the docsite and runs down common use including the `buildgen.go`, the basic verbs and the nature of the hidden workspace and the ability to expose these via `go` and `go-env` goals.  This can serve as an example for each backend as well - they all should have a well-contained README for the practitioner of that backend - java, scala, python, go, ... - to get them up and running using pants to build their code.

issue_title: Improve Options parsing @rules with engine I/O for file_option and dir_option
issue_body:
a TODO spawned from #5831. these currently require direct I/O, but should use the engine.

@stuhood was thinking we could just ban paths outside of the buildroot to make this easier. I had similar thoughts but wasn't sure if that would fly long term (e.g. `/tmp` would be off limit).

issue_title: Declare v2 products on v1 Tasks
issue_body:
aka "Remove requirement for graph manipulation tasks to fully hydrate a BuildGraph"

----

Currently, all pants Tasks (including things like `list`, `filter`, etc) implicitly require a complete BuildGraph to be hydrated.

To avoid actually walking the entire filesystem in order to hydrate that graph for tasks that don't examine source globs, the v1 engine uses lazily expanded `LazyFilesetWithSpec` objects. The downside to this laziness is that it cannot be cached in the daemon, so the v2 engine eagerly expands globs and creates `EagerFilesetWithSpec` objects, but this means that all file fingerprinting happens eagerly in the v2 engine.

----

The root cause is that Tasks are not specific about which products they need in order to run: instead, they implicitly require a `BuildGraph`. `list`, for example, only requires `Addresses` for the target spec roots, which are very cheap to compute. And `filter` only requires un-hydrated target objects (in the current v2 Rule datamodel, this means it only needs `UnhydratedStructs`).

Exposing a new API from Task that allows it to explicitly declare what v2 products it needs (perhaps with a default implementation that indicates that the Task requires a complete BuildGraph) would allow the v2 engine codepaths to avoid instantiating a BuildGraph for tasks that do not require it. It's also possible that Tasks should be able to expose v2 `Rules` that can assist in calculating its product requirements.

issue_title: Port isolated test invocations to the `IsolatedProcess` API
issue_body:
#4586 describes isolating test invokes so that for N test targets, we support invoking pex and/or junit N times.

The next step with regard to remoting is to port those process invokes to the [IsolatedProcess](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/tests/python/pants_test/engine/test_isolated_process.py) API (and adapting that API: see below), which is defined in terms of:
1. a series of input `Snapshot` objects,
2. arguments
3. a definition of how to capture the process output as a `Snapshot`

----

As part of this work, it may be necessary or helpful to tackle #4397: `Snapshots` are currently implemented on the rust side of the engine, but the `IsolatedProcess` API is mostly implemented on the python side.

issue_title: move extension/modification of process argv in the native backend into `Executable` subclassing instead of in each task
issue_body:
In #5815, we introduce the `NativeCompile` base task class to allow the `CCompile` and `CppCompile` tasks to specify the differences in their compilation process as *declaratively* as possible, with the class-level fields `source_target_constraint` and `workunit_label`, and the instance methods, `get_compile_settings()`, `get_compiler()`, and `get_compile_argv()`.

The last two of these methods should be merged into `get_compiler()` by moving the language-specific argument (e.g. `-x c++`) generation logic into the "compiler" object itself -- I believe this will be a first step to a clearer and more extensible process to support a myriad of compilation and linking options, which may be platform-specific, or require other logic which can be isolated from the tasks that invoke each process.

The `get_compiler()` method would likely accept a `NativeCompileSettings` subsystem instance, or whatever the v2 engine equivalent would be.

issue_title: Accept source_root definitions in pants.ini
issue_body:
Today these are defined in BUILD files which must then be registered as `--goals-bootstrap-buildfiles` in pants.ini.  This is a 2 step process that is confusing to the end user, and its an abuse of BUILD files to store non-target-graph configuration data.

For large organizations with - for example - multi-module maven project layouts, this list will get very large (>100s of entries).  In these cases something like #2143 will be needed such that this could be the configuration:
```ini
source_roots: @%(buildroot)s/source_roots.cfg
```
