issue_title: Add / fix support for text read mode
issue_body:
```
In [17]: lz4.frame.open(input_file, mode='rt', newline='', encoding='utf-8')
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
ValueError                                Traceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-17-ca68d3c17eba> in <module>()
----> 1 lz4.frame.open(input_file, mode='rt', newline='', encoding='utf-8')

/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/lz4/frame/__init__.py in open(filename, mode, encoding, errors, newline, block_size, block_linked, compression_level, content_checksum, block_checksum, auto_flush, return_bytearray, source_size)
    842         block_checksum=block_checksum,
    843         auto_flush=auto_flush,
--> 844         return_bytearray=return_bytearray,
    845     )
    846

/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/lz4/frame/__init__.py in __init__(self, filename, mode, block_size, block_linked, compression_level, content_checksum, block_checksum, auto_flush, return_bytearray, source_size)
    513             self._pos = 0
    514         else:
--> 515             raise ValueError('Invalid mode: {!r}'.format(mode))
    516
    517         if sys.version_info > (3, 6):

ValueError: Invalid mode: 'rt'
```

Looking at the source, it looks like mode is passed from https://github.com/python-lz4/python-lz4/blob/603c112a2c8dbaaa706780a94003aeb6888e36bb/lz4/frame/__init__.py#L837 to https://github.com/python-lz4/python-lz4/blob/603c112a2c8dbaaa706780a94003aeb6888e36bb/lz4/frame/__init__.py#L500

open() is happy with a mode of 'rt' and it passes it along unchanged to the LZ4FrameFile class which does not like 'rt'. Perhaps the mode should be changed to 'rb' when passing to the __init__ method, or perhaps the __init__ method should be happy with 'rt'.

Either way it breaks.

issue_title: Problem with misusing `__init__` methods of _speedups.c objects
issue_body: Repeated calling `__init__` method of scanner or encoder objects from `_speedups.c` causes memory leaks. Using scanner or encoder objects created with `__new__`, without calling `__init__`, causes a core dump. See CPython issue: http://bugs.python.org/issue30243. I think the solution of this issue is applicable to simplejson.

issue_title: Feature request: Bitrate information
issue_body:
### Checklist

- [ ] This is a bug report.
- [x] This is a feature request.
- [ ] This is a plugin (improvement) request.
- [ ] I have read the contribution guidelines.

### Description
I would like any way to check how much worse an alt stream is compared to the best stream.

### Expected / Actual behavior
Explained above.

### Comments, logs, screenshots, etc.
One option would be to add a command --showbitrate that adds the bitrate after each stream.
So it would be 720p (4400kbps), 720p60 (5000kbps), 720p_alt (3000kbps), 720p60_alt (3600kbps).

It would technically be pretties if it was just a percentage. Like streamlink finds the highest bitrate for quality X, all alt streams are represented as Xalt (%ofXbitrate). So 720p (5000) and 720p_alt (4000) would show 720p (best), 720p_alt (80%), since the alt stream has 80% of the bitrate of the best stream in that quality.

issue_title: Why is the entire dataset not cached by the end of first epoch?
issue_body:
Hi,
I ran the imagent-resent.py [https://github.com/tensorpack/tensorpack/blob/master/examples/ResNet/imagenet-resnet.py] from tensorpack examples using the below command.
"./imagenet-resnet.py --data /disk1/raw-data/ -d 50"

At the end of the first epoch, the cache size is around 85 GB (less than 145 GB imagenet data size)
Even after the second epoch has started, read ops are going to ssd.(at end of second epoch cache size is 130 GB).

I collected the above stats using cachestat command. I confirmed reads are going to ssd(in 2nd epoch) using iostat.
Moreover, the cache hit ratio keeps increasing slowly to 70%  by the end of first epoch and then close to 100% by the end of 2nd epoch.

I have sufficient dram to cache entire dataset (> 200 GB).
2 GPUs (v100)
Tensorpack version is 0.8.5
TensorFlow version is 1.6.0
Python Version 2.7.12

Why is the entire dataset not cached by the end of first epoch?

issue_title: Unit tests for update_properties() and delete() assume incorrect return values
issue_labels: [{'id': 429586823, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/zhmcclient/python-zhmcclient/labels/area:%20test', 'name': 'area: test', 'color': '1d76db', 'default': False}, {'id': 429588767, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/zhmcclient/python-zhmcclient/labels/resolution:%20fixed', 'name': 'resolution: fixed', 'color': 'cccccc', 'default': False}, {'id': 429587708, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/zhmcclient/python-zhmcclient/labels/type:%20bug', 'name': 'type: bug', 'color': '84b6eb', 'default': False}]
issue_body: The unit tests for the `update_properties()` and `delete()` methods of most Python resource classes assume incorrect return values from these functions (from before one of the changes in this area). This applies to a number of these method calls, and one needs to investigate which ones exactly.

issue_title: npm/yarn installs wrong (outdated) 5.1 version
issue_body:
### Describe the problem

npm & yarn seem to believe that `5.1.0-8` is the latest version of the 5.1 SVG icon package series (`@fortawesome/free-solid-svg-icons` et.al), rather than `5.1.0-11` (the actual latest as of this time of writing).

This is likely the culprit behind the recent series of 'missing icon' reports (e.g. #13173 and #13130).

### What did you expect?

`npm install @fortawesome/free-solid-svg-icons` should install `5.1.0-11`, not `5.1.0-8`.

### What version and implementation are you using?

Version: 5.1.x
Browser and version: N/A

- [x] SVG with JS
- [ ] Web Fonts with CSS
- [ ] SVG Sprites
- [ ] On the Desktop

### Reproducible test case

Since it's an install thing, this isn't exactly fiddle-able, but thankfully it's dead-easy to reproduce:

- Create & enter a new directory.
- `npm install @fortawesome/free-solid-svg-icons`
- Observe that `5.1.0-8` is installed, rather than `5.1.0-11`.

Fairly self-explanatory, but it's here for the sake of completely filling out the bugreport form. :P

### Bug report checklist

- [x] I have filled out as much of the above information as I can
- [x] I have included a test case because my odds go _way_ up that the team can fix this when I do
- [x] I have [searched for existing issues](https://github.com/FortAwesome/Font-Awesome/issues) and to the best of my knowledge this is not a duplicate

Adding @charset "UTF-8"; to the top of the font-awesome.scss resolves the issue.

issue_title: The git tags newest version on GitHub repository is different from website
issue_body:
Hi Font-Awesome team members,
I'm a member of [cdnjs](https://github.com/cdnjs/cdnjs). We hosted this [library](https://github.com/cdnjs/cdnjs/tree/master/ajax/libs/font-awesome).

I saw the library release v5.0.1 & v5.0.0 on the [website](https://fontawesome.com/how-to-use/upgrading-from-4), such as `https://use.fontawesome.com/releases/v5.0.0/js/all.js`.
But there is no v5.0.0 and v5.0.1 of git tag from GitHub repo.
The git auto-updater in [cdnjs](https://github.com/cdnjs/cdnjs) relies on [**git tags**](https://github.com/FortAwesome/Font-Awesome/releases) so that it can recognize the correct version and automatically update the repo.

Would you mind adding git tag or telling me your new git repository that CDNJS's bot can add the new versions of this awesome library?

Sorry for bothering you.
If you have any questions or concerns, please let me know.

Many thanks.

cdnjs/cdnjs#12307

issue_title: SFTP False error raised during open
issue_body:
Using git version (master) of the `ssh2-python`, the sftp open code doesn't handle the `LIBSSH2_ERROR_EAGAIN` and raise it as an error (since c2e5381bb4dffa1632219310277e96529f88a516)

I made a small patch which fix the issue on my local setup, which was raising all the time an error when trying to use sftp, because of the `LIBSSH2_ERROR_EAGAIN`, but this is probably but the most efficient way you want to commit (this is why I didn't made any Pull Request).

The patch: https://github.com/maxux/ssh2-python/commit/7dc38339f70bf7af848ee5e6f33345feb20043dd

Based on the C exemple here: http://yoyakuweb.com/lib/libssh2-1.4.3/libssh2-1.4.3/example/sftp_nonblock.c
Some `waitsocket` workaround should be done and not a loop like me.

Anyway, the `LIBSSH2_ERROR_EAGAIN` should definitively be handled during sftp open call.

issue_title: Bulk upsert with pk fields
issue_body:
Hi,

thanks for django-manager-utils  and query-builder: both have a very nice, clean API and are very helpful!

I've come across a weird case where I have a custom (CharField) primary key and want to do a (postgres) native bulk_upsert on that. That isn't currently possible because of this line in `querybuilder/query.py`:

`all_fields = [field for field in ModelClass._meta.fields if field.column != pk_name]`

(https://github.com/ambitioninc/django-query-builder/blob/develop/querybuilder/query.py#L1198)

Is there a specific reasons why PKs aren't allowed here? Maybe because of some implementation detail of AutoFields? From some cursory testing, bulk upserts work just fine with custom PKs. I don't have a complete demonstration case, but it could look something like this:

    class PersonModel(models.Model):
        name = models.CharField(max_length=40, primary_key=True)

    bulk_upsert(User.objects.all(),
        [User(name=str(i)) for i in range(100)],
        ['name'],
        native=True)

issue_title: Batchrunner report creation error when values are one of dict/list/ndarray
issue_body:
in batchrunner.py:
`ordered[param] = self.fixed_parameters[param]`

When values are a dict/list/np.ndarray, the above line throws the error:
ValueError: Length of values does not match length of index

This is because pandas interprets these datatypes as multiple values to fill a column with.

I used the following solution:
```
val = self.fixed_parameters[param]
if (type(val) == dict) or (type(val) == np.ndarray) or (type(val) == list):
    ordered[param] = str(val)
else:
    ordered[param] = val
```

It does the job for me, but it doesn't account for potential other datatypes that may have the same problem.  So not a very clean solution.  Any ideas how to handle this better?

issue_title: Tools for finding scheduler-dependent "heigenbugs"
issue_labels: [{'id': 523592585, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/python-trio/trio/labels/design%20discussion', 'name': 'design discussion', 'color': '0052cc', 'default': False}, {'id': 611722171, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/python-trio/trio/labels/pytest-trio%20relevant', 'name': 'pytest-trio relevant', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 537057261, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/python-trio/trio/labels/user%20happiness', 'name': 'user happiness', 'color': '0e8a16', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
Race conditions suck, but they're a common kind of bug in concurrent programs. We should help people test for them if we can.

Some interesting research out of MSR:

* [CHESS](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/publication/finding-and-reproducing-heisenbugs-in-concurrent-programs/)
* [GAMBIT](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/publication/gambit-effective-unit-testing-for-concurrency-libraries/)

CHESS assumes you have a preemptive multitasking system and works by hooking synchronization primitives, and explores different legal paths through the resulting happens-before graph. This makes it tricky to find data races, i.e., places where two threads access the same variable without using any synchronization primitive; you really want to be able to try schedules where threads get preempted in the middle of these. In practice, it sounds like the way they do this (see page 7) is to first run some heavyweight data race detector that instruments memory reads and writes, and then use the result to add new annotations for the CHESS scheduler.

For us, we don't AFAIK have any reasonable way to do data race detection in Python (maybe you could write something with PyPy? it's non-trivial), but we do have direct access to the scheduler, so we can in principle explore all possible preemption decisions directly instead of having to infer them from synchronization points. However, this is likely to be somewhat inefficient – for CHESS it *really* needs to cut down the space of preemption points because otherwise it has to consider a preemption at every instruction which is ridiculously intractable, and we're in a better position then that, but the average program does still have lots of cases where two tasks that aren't interacting at all have some preemption points and this generates an exponential space of boring possible schedules.

They have a lot of interesting stuff in the paper (section 4) about how to structure a search, recovering from non-determinism in the code-under-test, some (not enough) detail about fair scheduling (they want to handle code with spin-locks). It looks like [this is the fair scheduling paper](https://pdfs.semanticscholar.org/adec/c5f64d69031ee5048bac09d1da1fc7811e75.pdf); it looks like the main idea is that if a thread calls `sched_yield` or equivalent then it means it can't make progress, so you should lower its priority. That's... not a good signal in the trio context. Fortunately it's not clear that anyone wants to write spin-locks anyway... (we use the equivalent a few times in our test suite, but mostly only in the earliest tests I wrote before we had much infrastructure, and certainly it's a terrible thing to do in real code).

Possibly useful citation: "ConTest [15] is a lightweight testing tool that attempts to create scheduling variance without resorting to systematic generation of all executions. In contrast, CHESS obtains greater control over thread scheduling to offer higher coverage guarantees and better reproducibility."

A lot of the cleverness in GAMBIT is about figuring out which states are equivalent and thus can be collapsed 

I suspect there are two general approaches that are likely to be most useful in our context:

* Hypothesis-style randomized exploration of the space of scheduling decisions, with some clever heuristics to guide the search towards edge cases that are likely to find bugs, and maybe even hypothesis-style minimization. (The papers above have some citations to other papers showing that low-preemption traces are often sufficient to demonstrate bugs in practice.)

* Providing some sort of mechanism for users to explicitly guide the exploration to a subset of cases that they've identified as a problem (either by intuition or especially for regression tests and increasing coverage). You can imagine relatively simple things like "use these priorities until task 3 hits the yield point on line 72 and then switch to task 5", maybe?

An intermediate form between these is one of the heuristics mentioned in the GAMBIT paper, of letting the programmer name some specific functions they want to "focus" on, and using that to increase the number of preemptions that happen within those functions. (This is sort of mentioned in the CHESS paper too when talking about their state space reduction techniques.)


Possibly implementation for scheduler control: call an instrument hook before scheduling a batch, with a list of runnable tasks (useful as a general notification anyway), and let them optionally return something to control the scheduling of this batch.

issue_title: Speed up runtest.py

issue_title: Wrong date parsing when year changes
issue_body:
During scraping a [website](http://www.dzone.com/links/index.html) I encountered this issue:

Dec 14 11:00 is parsed as datetime.datetime(2015, 12, 14, 11, 0) whereas it was supposed to mean datetime.datetime(2014, 12, 14, 11, 0) because it was a post of 2014.

I think there should be parameter like *only_allow_past_dates* which should disable future date parsing and interpret it only as the date that has passed.

issue_title: Module parameters (and module outputs?) with named lists and vectors
issue_body:
I can see this being useful in many cases (but not essential).

For example, currently I have module definition

```yaml
t: R(mu <- par[[1]]; df <- par[[2]]; x <- mu + rt(n,df))
  par: R(c(mu = 0,df = 2)),
       R(c(mu = 1,df = 2)),
       R(c(mu = 2,df = 4))
  n: 100
  $data: x
  $true_mean: 3
```

But it would be nice to have:

```yaml
t: R(x <- par$mu + rt(n,par$df))
  par: R(c(mu = 0,df = 2)),
       R(c(mu = 1,df = 2)),
       R(c(mu = 2,df = 4))
  n: 100
  $data: x
  $true_mean: 3
```

DSC removes the names of the vector/list elements.

Again, not essential, but something to consider.

issue_title: Emulation exception handler is overly broad
issue_body:
### OS / Environment
Ubuntu 16.04

### Manticore version
`master`

### Python version
2.7

### Summary of the problem
Instruction emulation with Unicorn needs to concretize all registers, since Unicorn is a purely concrete emulator. When a register contains symbolic data, `emulate` raises `ConcretizeRegister` to obtain a solution. However, the [exception handler](https://github.com/trailofbits/manticore/blob/77b0542b/manticore/core/cpu/abstractcpu.py#L854) for Unicorn catches all exceptions and wraps them into `InstructionEmulationError`s.

### Step to reproduce the behavior
```
user@ubuntu /m/h/p/manticore> cat ret.c
#include <unistd.h>
int main(void) {
    int ret;
    read(0, &ret, sizeof ret);
    return ret;
}
user@ubuntu /m/h/p/manticore> gcc -static ret.c -o ret
user@ubuntu /m/h/p/manticore> manticore ret
2017-12-01 08:08:30,397: [8804] m.manticore:INFO: Loading program ret
2017-12-01 08:08:31,397: [8804] m.manticore:INFO: Starting 1 processes.
2017-12-01 08:08:35,585: [8804] m.c.executor:ERROR: Exception:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/mnt/hgfs/projects/manticore/manticore/core/executor.py", line 460, in run
    if not current_state.execute():
  File "/mnt/hgfs/projects/manticore/manticore/core/state.py", line 126, in execute
    result = self._platform.execute()
  File "/mnt/hgfs/projects/manticore/manticore/platforms/linux.py", line 2007, in execute
    self.current.execute()
  File "/mnt/hgfs/projects/manticore/manticore/core/cpu/abstractcpu.py", line 820, in execute
    self.emulate(insn)
  File "/mnt/hgfs/projects/manticore/manticore/core/cpu/abstractcpu.py", line 857, in emulate
    raise InstructionEmulationError(str(e))
InstructionEmulationError

issue_title: importCell() function can modify h variables (eg. h.celsius)
issue_body:
- imporCell() loads .hoc or .py file with cell definition; if this file modifies the h context (eg. h.celsius), it is modified in the netpyne model

issue_body:
http://opencobolide.readthedocs.io/en/latest/advanced.html#setup-a-custom-compiler-on-windows points to Sergey's old VC builds - while it is currently preferred to build the environment on your own (everything needed is in build_windows directory of the windows source zip) there is a "newer" (than Sergey's) "official" testing build available at https://sourceforge.net/projects/open-cobol/files/gnu-cobol/2.0/gnu-cobol-2.0_nightly_r697_only_for_testing_purposes_win32_and_x64_vc11_bin.7z/download

It would be better to point people there and use this for testing than the other version. I'll drop another note here as soon as an official release binary package for VC is available.

issue_title: [Bug] File system view is reloaded even when switching between files with same file path
issue_body:
I've opened two files from the same directory. When switching between them the "file system view" is refreshed. As the files are on a network share with lots of files in the directory this needs some time.

The good workaround is to lock the view.

- PyQt: 5.4.1
- pyqode.core: 2.6.1

issue_title: RelatedFieldWidgetWrapper raises KeyError on Django
issue_body:
Affects: Django 1.11.0 but not >=1.11.1 (i never tested <1.10. *may* affect early 2.0.)

Problem: Trying to create or view a ProductClass in th Dashboard results in a KeyError on context['field'].

Bug description
-------------------
`RelatedFieldWidgetWrapper` in apps/dashboard/widgets.py: inherits from Widget but does not call super().\_\_init__.  
This means that some variables that `Widget.get_context()` builds are not present in `RelatedFieldWidgetWrapper.get_context()` (context['widget']).

When rendering ProductClass form in the dashboard, the template calls widget_tweaks's `render_field` with a class attribute added. When adding class attributes, widget tweaks will test the `BoundField` for truthyness.
In Django==1.11.0 , `BoundField` is only has \_\_len__ (which is used for truthyness tests) which eventually leads to accessing context['widget'] (which is not created). In later versions (after [this commit](https://github.com/django/django/commit/844ae40a7cdcb1738ca3a2e8f13bb6b8c2e22efd) ) BoundField got a `__bool__()` method so no access to subwidgets happens.

Solution 1: Change `RelatedFieldWidgetWrapper` to call super().\_\_init__. I don't quite understand it entirely and maybe there is a reason it doesn't, so i'm not submitting a PR
Solution 2: Require >Django==1.11.0

issue_title: use a dictionary for query params
issue_body:
`hyperlink` does not support dictionaries for `query`. while i know that technically this is an `orderedmultidict`, this is just not nice from an api perspective:

``` python
>>> url = URL.from_text('https://example.org/api/v2')

>>> url.replace(query={'foo': 'bar'})
[...]
ValueError: too many values to unpack (expected 2)
```

instead, this works:

``` python
>>> url.replace(query=[('foo', 'bar')])
URL.from_text('https://example.org/api/v2?foo=bar')
```

or using `.items()`:

```python
>>> url.replace(query={'foo': 'bar'}.items())
URL.from_text('https://example.org/api/v2?foo=bar')
```

but tbh none of this looks appealing to me. :)

any reason for the current behaviour?

issue_body:
Greetings,

I am using the linux_i2c_userspace HAL with the 508A chip. I've update the 508A configuration struct to match my slave address of 0x60 and bus of 1. When running the cmd-processor, I submit the "508" command to select the desired config. When I then execute the "sernum" command I am presented with an error: "atcab_read_serial_number() failed with ret=0x000000E7". I figured I'd start with something basic before even attempting the unit tests.

I traced this to the HAL's wakeup function. The read function is returning -1 and thus returning a status of ATCA_RX_NO_RESPONSE. i2cdetect does find my device at 0x60. Our device tree file has the i2c bus setup for the standard 100kHz. 

Thank you for any guidance you can provide.

issue_title: Auto-include does not ignore #example# or example~ files
issue_body:
e.g. Tar-ball contains temp files from common Unix editors:

```
$ planemo shed_upload --tar_only  ~/repositories/pico_galaxy/tools/nlstradamus/ && tar -xvf shed_upload.tar.gz
cp '/tmp/tmpDhxzFT' 'shed_upload.tar.gz'
#README.rst#
README.rst~
README.rst
nlstradamus.xml
tool_dependencies.xml
```

Either planemo needs some additional default excludes, or perhaps it could read the local ``.gitignore`` to expand the exclude list? (I would have said that was too magic, but planemo seems to do a lot of clever stuff like this already)

issue_title: planemo dependency_script can loose current directory
issue_body:
e.g. https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/blob/master/packages/package_samtools_1_2/tool_dependencies.xml has:

```xml
...
                    <action type="shell_command">cd htslib-1.2.1 && make bgzip tabix</action>
                    <action type="move_file">
                        <source>htslib-1.2.1/bgzip</source>
                        <destination>$INSTALL_DIR/bin</destination>
                    </action>
                    <action type="move_file">
                        <source>htslib-1.2.1/tabix</source>
                        <destination>$INSTALL_DIR/bin</destination>
                    </action>
...
```

When run via Galaxy, `` <action type="shell_command">cd htslib-1.2.1 && make bgzip tabix</action>`` has no lasting change on the current directory.

Currently via ``planemo dependency_script`` use of  ``cd htslib-1.2.1`` is never reverted (so the later steps break).

Workaround:

```xml
 <action type="shell_command">cd htslib-1.2.1 && make bgzip tabix && cd ..</action>
```

Probably the simplest fix is to append ``cd $dep_install_tmp`` to the conversion of any  ``<action type="shell_command">`` step?

issue_title: Support for Route Record Indicator pavkets
issue_body:
When using the Python Xbee library in callback mode the callback thread crashes if it receives an API Route Record Indicator (type a1) packet.

This then renders the python program dead as it is unable to process any further received packets.

1. Add supports to the Zigbee API dictionary for Route Record Indicator type packets.

2. Look st whether it is really necessary to break (therefore crash CB thread) if the library receives a packet type it does not know - should it rather just warn?

Jim

issue_title: Don't use "unittest2" for Python 3
issue_body:
linecache2 requires unittest2 for testing, but unittest2 "is no longer needed" for Python 3 (quoted from https://pypi.python.org/pypi/unittest2). Please update the requirements file to conditionally require unittest2 only for older Python version.

Each additional package makes distribution maintainers life more complicated, since they have to maintain yet another package even if the distribution only includes Python 3.5 or even 3.6.

Thanks.

issue_title: Tokens from file: new file name pattern
issue_body:
New token spec:

```yaml
apiVersion: "zalando.org/v1"
kind: PlatformCredentialsSet
metadata:
   name: my-app-credentials
spec:
   application: my-app
   tokens:
     full-access:
       privileges:
         - com.zalando::foobar.write
         - com.zalando::acme.full
     read-only:
       privileges:
         - com.zalando::foobar.read
 ```

becomes:

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: my-app-credentials
type: Opaque
data:
  full-access-token-type: Bearer
  full-access-token-secret: JwAbc123.. # JWT token
  read-only-token-type: Bearer
  read-only-token-secret: JwBcd456.. # JWT token
```

Note the new "-token-secret" suffix.

issue_title: non-endpoint flags do not work with Endpoint handlers
issue_body:
While an argument could be made that the following is poor style, it is surprising that the config_changed handler fails:

```python
class GRPCEndpoint(reactive.Endpoint):
    @when('endpoint.{endpoint_name}.joined')
    def joined(self):
        self.publish()
        reactive.clear_flag(self.expand_name('endpoint.{endpoint_name}.joined'))

    @when('config.changed')
    def config_changed(self):
        self.publish()

    def publish(self):
        config = hookenv.config()
        for rel in self.relations:
            rel.to_publish['target'] = config['target']
            rel.to_publish['ca'] = config['ca']
            rel.to_publish['timeout'] = config['timeout']
```

The traceback would not help people diagnose the problem:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File ".../.venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/charms/reactive/__init__.py", line 72, in main
    bus.dispatch(restricted=restricted_mode)
  File ".../.venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/charms/reactive/bus.py", line 382, in dispatch
    _invoke(other_handlers)
  File ".../.venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/charms/reactive/bus.py", line 358, in _invoke
    handler.invoke()
  File ".../.venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/charms/reactive/bus.py", line 180, in invoke
    self._action(*args)
TypeError: config_changed() missing 1 required positional argument: 'self'
```

Work around is to do things as intended, providing methods of the endpoint to publish data and driving it from a normal handler in the main charm.

issue_title: Removed cookie handling
issue_body:
I think there's a problem with removing cookies: if we get them via ``splash:get_cookies``, then if the cookie is set to an empty string (as a result of logout), it will be absent in ``response.data['cookies']``, and as a result, they will stay in the ``cookiejar`` in ``SplashCookiesMiddleware``: https://github.com/lopuhin/scrapy-splash/commit/478db5d729adb027c43d72915c98aacb75bbbc64

I see three ways to fix this:

1. if the cookie disappeared from ``response.data['cookies']``, remove it from the ``cookiejar`` too
2. if the cookie disappeared from ``response.data['cookies']``, add it to ``cookiejar`` with an empty string value?
3. get cookies in some other way, for example from ``har`` log?

Not sure which way is the best. (1) is by far the easiest and looks very logical, but this might be different from what people will expect?

issue_title: GET never used
issue_body:
I was looking through middleware.py and see this: ``` def process_request(self, request, spider): if 'splash' not in request.meta: return if request.method not in {'GET', 'POST'}: logger.warn( "Currently only GET and POST requests are supported by " "SplashMiddleware; %(request)s will be handled without Splash", {'request': request}, extra={'spider': spider} ) return request .... new_request = request.replace( url=splash_url, method='POST', body=body, headers=headers, priority=request.priority + self.rescheduling_priority_adjust ) self.crawler.stats.inc_value('splash/%s/request_count' % endpoint) return new_request ``` This means any requests sent from scrapy to splash will be POST requests. This is confimed in the splash logs. Problem I have is when CURL sends something to Splash, it uses a GET and the JavaScript is executed and the page is returned correctly. issue_title: __repr__ should return str in Py2
issue_body:
Hi all! Thanks for maintaining project!

`__repr__` in bundle should return str function in Py2. It's standard python-behavior. And some programs may rely on that fact.
https://docs.python.org/2/reference/datamodel.html#object.__repr__

Also, I have problem in one Django-project with representation of bundles. My bundle.data can contain dictionary with models. Models have defined `__unicode__`. But Django under the hood convert `__unicode__`-result to ascii in `__repr__` and `__str__` functions. And calling `__repr__` inside dictionary while representing bundle caused `UnicodeDecodeError`.

Example:
```
bundle.data = [
    {
        'model': my_model  # repr(my_model) will return str, not unicode
    }
]

repr(bundle) # calling __repr__ for my_model, it's Python's behavior
             # UnicodeDecodeError
```

Thanks!

    var i = 0

    val gradOutput = Tensor[Double](2, 4, 6, 5)

    val start = System.nanoTime()
    val output = layer.forward(input)
    val gradInput = layer.backward(input, gradOutput)
    val end = System.nanoTime()
    val scalaTime = end - start

    val code = "torch.manualSeed(" + seed + ")\n" +
      """module = nn.CMul(1, 4, 1, 1)
        output = module:forward(input)
        gradInput = module:backward(input,gradOutput)
        gradWeight = module.gradWeight"""

    val (luaTime, torchResult) = TH.run(code, Map("input" -> input, "gradOutput" -> gradOutput),
      Array("output", "gradInput", "gradWeight"))
    val luaOutput = torchResult("output").asInstanceOf[Tensor[Double]]
    val luaGradInput = torchResult("gradInput").asInstanceOf[Tensor[Double]]
    val luaGradWeight = torchResult("gradWeight").asInstanceOf[Tensor[Double]]

    output should be (luaOutput)
    gradInput should be (luaGradInput)
    layer.gradWeight should be (luaGradWeight)

    println("Test case : CMul, Torch : " + luaTime + " s, Scala : " + scalaTime / 1e9 + " s")
  }
```

issue_title: Database password configuration issue for installation
issue_body:
I've been following the INSTALL.rst instructions for installation of the pu branch.

When setting the database password (CFG_DATABASE_PASS) in the configuration, either through inveniomanage or via manual editing of the cfg file, there is an inconsistency.

If the password is set as password with no quotes, this command fails:
```inveniomanage database init --user=root --password=$MYSQL_ROOT --yes-i-know```
Since the resulting SQL is invalid without the password wrapped in quotes.

When the password is wrapped in quotes, this command will work.
However, now the below command will fail to work
```inveniomanage database create```

So to get this working, you have to edit the config file again and remove the quotes, leaving u'password' in place.

issue_title: Travis: fix test failures
tests: simplify doctest execution
issue_body:
The following cookiecutter change:

   https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98

should be propagated to this Invenio module.

Namely, in `run-tests.sh`, the sphinx for doctests is invoked after pytest run:

```console
$ tail -3 ./\{\{\ cookiecutter.project_shortname\ \}\}/run-tests.sh
sphinx-build -qnNW docs docs/_build/html && python setup.py test && sphinx-build -qnNW -b doctest docs docs/_build/doctest
```

This sometimes led to problems on Travis CI with the second sphinx-build run due
to "disappearing" dependencies after the example application was tested.

A solution that worked for invenio-marc21 (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49#issuecomment-281287103)
and that was integrated in cookiecutter (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98) was to
run doctest execution in pytest, removing the second `sphinx-build` invocation.

This both solved Travis CI build failures and simplified test suite execution.

Note that this change may necessitate to amend the code tests etc so that things
would be executed with the Flask application context (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49/commits/09e98fc18fd5591dc65d806286e8da51b7e384b4).

issue_body:
Hi,  I am trying to understand the use of flex-grow in the layouts of Jupyter Widgets and I think I have found a bug. ```python
from ipywidgets import Layout, Button, Box

items = [
    Button(description='weight=1'),

    Button(description='weight=1'),
 ]

                    border='solid',
                    width='50%')

box
```

issue_title: KeyError when querying bug data from Bugzilla version 3.x
issue_body:
I'm trying to get bug information from several Bugzilla instances, namely:

    In [12]: for url in urls:
       ....:        bz = Bugzilla(url)
       ....:        print(url, bz.bz_ver_major, bz.bz_ver_minor)
       ....: 
    ('https://bugzilla.novell.com', 3, 4)
    ('https://bugs.gentoo.org', 3, 0)
    ('https://bugs.mageia.org', 4, 4)
    ('https://bugzilla.redhat.com', 4, 4)

When trying `bz.getbug(123)` on Novell's and Gentoo's Bugzilla instance, I hit the else branch of [this](https://github.com/python-bugzilla/python-bugzilla/blob/0b2397db551b8969fa7b71a15cf2ff80d3146437/bugzilla/base.py#L1032) check that tries to access `b['internals']`. This attribute does not exist in the bug dictionary so it fails with a `KeyError: 'internals'` traceback.

If I remove that check for the BZ version altogether, all seems to work just fine. Was the `internals` used at some point and has been removed now? I'd submit a patch removing that check but would like to see first if it's actually used for anything. I can't seem to find any `internals` attribute exposed by any Bugzilla instances.

Thanks!

issue_title: readthedocs points to an unreleased version
issue_body:
I spent the better part of this day trying to figure out why my installation of marshmallow didn't have the constants INCLUDE, RAISE, etc.  After some digging, I figured out what the problem was: the default readthedocs link points to documentation for the yet unreleased dev version of the package. 

I think this should definitely be fixed or the docs should state the discrepancy in a more visible manner. At the very least, it should be made clear that the `$ pip install -U marshmallow --pre` under the `Get It Now` section` does not point to the same version which the documents describe.

issue_title: Errored fields should not appear in output data
issue_body:
```python
from marshmallow import Schema, fields, validate as v

class MySchema(Schema):
    foo = fields.Str(validate=v.Length(min=3))
sch = MySchema()

res = sch.load({'foo': 'a'})
print(res.errors)  # {'foo': ['Shorter than minimum length 3.']}
```

### Current

```python
print(res.data)  # {'foo': None}
```

### Proposed

Errored fields will not be included in the output dict.

```python
print(res.data)  # {}
```


This was discussed briefly in #153.

issue_title: Denormalize on deserialization
Support Enum for Select-field
issue_body:
I have got some use cases where I use a `Enum` in my models.

Example:
```python
# definitions.py
from enum import Enum

class Gender(Enum):
    male = 'm'
    female = 'f'
```

```python
# models.py
from definitions import Gender

class Person:
    def __init__(self, gender: Gender):
        self.gender = gender
```

Now it would be great if I could use the Enum in `marshmallow.fields.Select`:
```python
# schemas.py
from marshmallow import fields, Schema
from definitions import Gender
from models import Person

class PersonSchema(Schema):
    gender = fields.Select(Gender)

    @staticmethod
    def make_object(data) -> Person:
        return Person(**data)
```

For backwards-compatibility [enum34](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/enum34) could be used.

issue_title: Data loss when fetching from S3/HTTP/HTTPS
issue_body:
Using pysam 0.11.2.2 linked against libcurl (version tbd), I was fetching 4,000 BCF files via signed S3 HTTPS URLs one chromosome at a time by specifying both BCF and index URLs.  A very few number of those fetches failed, but either due to a failure in htslib or in pysam, the failure was not retried until success nor was the error was not propagated to the calling code.  This failure led to silent data loss.

I'm going to investigate more deeply and request clarification from the htslib developers on how error and retry handling are performed when htslib is linked against libcurl.

issue_title: TLEN vs query_alignment_length
issue_body:
Just wondering if there was something in the BAM format that suggests if you should return the read length when there is no paired read and TLEN == 0?

Example:
`samtools`
```bash
samtools view SRR1585557_sort_resort.bam chr10:138870-138900
$ SRR1585557.11734192     16      chr10   138872  25      21M     *       0       0       GAGTTGATGGTCTAGCGATTG   `RRJ^[bZeb[_Q``Q\Sc_^   XT:A:U  NM:i:2  X0:i:1  X1:i:0  XM:i:2  XO:i:0      XG:i:0  MD:Z:11G4C4
```
`pysam`
```python
import pysam
b = pysam.AlignmentFile('SRR1585557_sort_resort.bam' ,'rb')
print(next(b.fetch('chr10',138870-138900)))
>>>SRR1585557.11734192	16	0	138871	25	21M	-1	-1	21	GAGTTGATGGTCTAGCGATTG	array('B', [63, 49, 49, 41, 61, 58, 65, 57, 68, 65, 58, 62, 48, 63, 63, 48, 59, 50, 66, 62, 61])	[('XT', 'U'), ('NM', 2), ('X0', 1), ('X1', 0), ('XM', 2), ('XO', 0), ('XG', 0), ('MD', '11G4C4')]

I know that samtools is doing something with regard to RNEXT and PNEXT, I was just wondering why Pysam reports the seq length and not 0.

issue_title: Preload option in RealGalaxyCatalog only preload HDUs not data
issue_body:
The preload option in RealGalaxyCatalog, and by extension, COSMOSCatalog does open all the files and reads in the HDUs but the data is not actually loaded into RAM. From what I can read in issue #168 this is by design, but I have a use case where I actually need to have the whole catalog loaded in memory (I need to very rapidly produce galaxy images in random order).
 
I can manually force the catalog to preload all the data with the following:
```
# Doesn't actually preload, returns very quickly
cat = galsim.COSMOSCatalog(preload=True)

# Takes a while as it cycles through the files, memory usage steadily increases in the process
for f in cat.real_cat.loaded_files:
    for h in cat.real_cat.loaded_files[f]:
        h.data;
```

But what to do in this situation ? Should there be separate preload and preload_HDU options ? 

If you need to preload the HDUs, most likely you are going to loop through the entire catalog which will progressively load the whole thing in memory anyway, so in terms of memory usage it doesn't change much. But preloading the catalog by sequentially reading all the files is much faster than randomly accessing different HDUs until the whole catalog is loaded.

issue_title: Partial decorator should be parameterised: do not save the token into the session
issue_body:
**Issue**
Saving the `partial token` into the session is not always a good thing to do.
[Done here](https://github.com/python-social-auth/social-core/blob/master/social_core/pipeline/partial.py#L35)

**Why**
For example: Google provides the feature to add other gmail accounts to your "default" account. When a user tries to authenticate with one of the accounts, but the pipeline is interrupted with a partial step, he/she cannot authenticate with another account, as long as the "first" `partial_token` is in the session.

**Propose**
 The decorator is supposed to be parametrised to be able to control whether saving the token into the session or not.

**Solution**
```
def partial(save_to_session=True):
    def decorator(func):
        @wraps(func)
        def wrapper(strategy, backend, pipeline_index, *args, **kwargs):
            current_partial = partial_prepare(strategy, backend, pipeline_index,
                                              *args, **kwargs)

            out = func(strategy=strategy,
                       backend=backend,
                       pipeline_index=pipeline_index,
                       current_partial=current_partial,
                       *args, **kwargs) or {}

            if not isinstance(out, dict):
                strategy.storage.partial.store(current_partial)
                if save_to_session:
                    strategy.session_set('partial_pipeline_token', current_partial.token)
            return out
        return wrapper
    return decorator
```
 
**WDYT?**
If you agree I can submit a PR with the "fix".

issue_title: Installing with pip -t broken
issue_body:
1. OS type and version
`Linux localhost.localdomain 4.11.11-300.fc26.x86_64 #1 SMP Mon Jul 17 16:32:11 UTC 2017 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux`

Rest of the questions are context sensitive.

If I install google-cloud in someEnv like so...

```bash

[stephen@localhost ~]$ python2.7 -m pip --version
pip 9.0.1 from /usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages (python 2.7)

[stephen@localhost ~]$ virtualenv someEnv
New python executable in /home/stephen/someEnv/bin/python2.7
Also creating executable in /home/stephen/someEnv/bin/python
Installing setuptools, pip, wheel...done.

[stephen@localhost ~]$ source someEnv/bin/activate
(someEnv) [stephen@localhost ~]$ python2.7 -m pip install google-cloud --upgrade
# ...snip...
(someEnv) [stephen@localhost ~]$ ls someEnv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google
api  auth  cloud  gapic  gax  iam  logging  longrunning  oauth2  protobuf  resumable_media  rpc  type

```
Those folders look good, right?

Now, lets say I want to use `cloud` with AppEngine Standard. You would normally use `-t some_target` with pip and vendor `some_target` in your appengine_config.py, right?

```bash
[stephen@localhost ~]$ mkdir myAppEngineProject
[stephen@localhost ~]$ python2.7 -m pip install google-cloud -t myAppEngineProject/lib --upgrade
# ...snip...
[stephen@localhost ~]$ ls myAppEngineProject/lib/google
protobuf
```

So only protobuf makes it through. Without the `--upgrade` switch, this happens:

```bash

[stephen@localhost ~]$ rm myAppEngineProject/lib/ -rf
[stephen@localhost ~]$ python2.7 -m pip install google-cloud -t myAppEngineProject/lib
# ...snip...
Target directory /home/stephen/myAppEngineProject/lib/google already exists. Specify --upgrade to force replacement.
[stephen@localhost ~]$ ls myAppEngineProject/lib/google
api  auth  cloud  gapic  gax  iam  logging  longrunning  oauth2  resumable_media  rpc  type

```
So now `protobuf ` is missing and pip just wants to wipe the whole google dir when upgrading, even from a fresh install.

This is also an issue with just wanting to install `google-cloud-pubsub`, guess other sub-projects might be affected.

issue_title: Storage: Downloads give incorrect not found error if object overwritten between calls
issue_labels: [{'id': 77678602, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20storage', 'name': 'api: storage', 'color': 'f8f8f8', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604243, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/priority:%20p2', 'name': 'priority: p2', 'color': 'fef2c0', 'default': False}, {'id': 77438584, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/type:%20question', 'name': 'type: question', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
Downloads from GCS currently seem to be accomplished as a two step process:

1. Fetch the current object's metadata, including a generation number.
2. Using the generation number returned in step 1, attempt to download the object.

Sample download showing an example of this:

```
client = storage.Client()
bucket = client.get_bucket('yarbrough-test')
blob = bucket.get_blob('test.txt')
blob.download_to_filename('/tmp/test.txt')
```

What this does:

GET /storage/v1/b/yarbrough-test?projection=noAcl
GET /storage/v1/b/yarbrough-test/o/test.txt
GET /download/storage/v1/b/yarbrough-test/o/test.txt?generation=1511999426608623&alt=media


The problem:

In the case where the object has been overwritten by a new generation between the metadata GET and the donwload GET, a "not found" error would be reported to the user even though at no point did an object with the specified name not exist. In other words, this happens:

Time 1: GET /storage/v1/b/bucket/o/object returns generation 12345.
Time 2: User in another thread creates a new generation of text.txt, generation 23456. Generation 12345 is deleted and now bucket "bucket" object "object" implies the new generation.
Time 3: GET /download/storage/v1/b/bucket/o/object?generation=12345 fails with a 404 because there is no longer a generation 12345.

Steps to reproduce:

1. Create a GCS bucket. Do NOT enable versioning.
2. Start a script which rapidly overwrites an object with a new generation.
3. Start a second script which repeatedly downloads the object.

You are likely to eventually run into a "404", although an object exists at all times.

How to fix:

I suggest this be fixed by retrying the download step if it returns with a 404 after the metadata is successfully fetched, unless the user explicitly asked for a particular generation number. Alternately, I might suggest skipping the metadata fetch step entirely, unless the user asks for some piece of metadata, which would significantly reduce the latency for small downloads.

issue_title: PubSub subscription.pull() fails silently in multiprocessing
issue_body:
* Python multiprocessing is broken by grpcio==0.1.6.
* Downgrading to grpcio==0.1.4 is a workaround.
* The 'spawn' context option _should_ be a workaround but is not because RLock objects are present and cannot be pickled for transmission to child processes.

1. OS type and version
MacOs and linux (Google App Engine Flex)
2. Python version and virtual environment information `python --version`
python 3.6.2
3. `pip freeze`
cachetools==2.0.1
certifi==2017.7.27.1
chardet==3.0.4
dill==0.2.7.1
future==0.16.0
gapic-google-cloud-pubsub-v1==0.15.4
google-auth==1.1.0
google-cloud-core==0.26.0
google-cloud-pubsub==0.27.0
google-gax==0.15.14
googleapis-common-protos==1.5.2
grpc-google-iam-v1==0.11.3
grpcio==1.6.0
httplib2==0.10.3
idna==2.6
monotonic==1.3
oauth2client==3.0.0
ply==3.8
proto-google-cloud-pubsub-v1==0.15.4
protobuf==3.4.0
pyasn1==0.3.5
pyasn1-modules==0.1.4
requests==2.18.4
rsa==3.4.2
six==1.11.0
tenacity==4.4.0
urllib3==1.22

4. Stacktrace if available
fails silently

5. Steps to reproduce
. `virtualenv -p python3 env`
. `pip install google-cloud-pubsub==0.27.0`
. `. env/bin/activate`
. create topic and subscription for example (see below)
. `python <example>.py`

6. Code example
```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

# Works with grpcio==0.1.4
# Fails silently with grpcio==0.1.6 (the default 2017-09-18)

from multiprocessing import Process

from google.cloud import pubsub


class MyClass(object):
    def __init__(self):
        c = pubsub.Client()
        t = c.topic('my-topic')  # created previously
        self._sub = t.subscription('my-sub')  # created previously

    def print_messages(self):
        print('starting worker...')
        
        while True:
            try:
                envelopes = self._sub.pull()
                
                if envelopes:
                    ack_id, message = envelopes[0]
                    
                    print(
                        'ack_id: {}\ndata: {}; attributes: {}'.format(
                            ack_id, message.data, message.attributes
                        )
                    )
                    
                    self._sub.acknowledge([ack_id])
            except KeyboardInterrupt:
                break
                
        print('shutdown worker.')
            

if __name__ == '__main__':
    my_class = MyClass()
    p = Process(target=my_class.print_messages)
    p.start()
    
    try:
        p.join()
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        print('shutdown parent.')
    
```

issue_title: Text Detection Client Not Synched Up to Server Update _KEY_MAP values Missing
issue_body:
Hi,

There seems to be some changes in JSON response returned from the server, but the client library is not up-to-date.

The issue seems to be from an outdated [annotations.py](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/8f3881adb20e0783599add7a3528e7aa69e1093e/vision/google/cloud/vision/annotations.py)

For instance, I see that there is now a key called fullTextAnnotation returned by the server when calling detect_text(), but the corresponding _KEY_MAP is missing this key in the client library I installed from pip, causing KeyError.  The latest commit from your repository does have this key, but as far as I understand these changes are not released yet?

Would there be a possibility of cross checking the versions in the future so that when the server changes it is still backwards compatible and doesn't just break the client?

issue_title: BigQuery: doesn't declare dependency on concurrent.futures
issue_body:
[bigquery/client.py imports concurrent.futures](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/09897f31f83023ae9059a0eb4296b15d4785678d/bigquery/google/cloud/bigquery/client.py#L20) but it doesn't declare the dependency in [setup.py](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/09897f31f83023ae9059a0eb4296b15d4785678d/bigquery/setup.py#L53). It should have a declaration like [the one from google-cloud-core==0.27.1](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/bee32c0ae68b1e6cf224f9017c758a8e31e8be83/core/setup.py#L63) (removed in 0.28.0).

Currently this is doesn't break anything because [api-core depends on futures](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/09897f31f83023ae9059a0eb4296b15d4785678d/api_core/setup.py#L62), but if that dependency disappears it will break.

This may be true for other clients, I haven't checked. I ran into it when looking at bazelbuild/rules_python#14.

issue_title: Some files are never closed
issue_body:
I'm working on a script using GitPython to loop over lots of Git repos, pull remote changes, etc...

At some point, I noticed that doing ``repo.is_dirty()`` was opening some files which never got closed until the process exits, which in turns causes the tool to crash with "too many open files" after iterating over enough repos:
```python
import os

import git

# Put here whatever Git repos you might have in the current folder
# Listing more than one makes the problem more visible
repos = ["ipset", "libhtp"]

raw_input("Check open files with `lsof -p %s | grep %s`" % (os.getpid(),
                                                            os.getcwd()))

for name in repos:
    repo = git.Repo(name)
    repo.is_dirty()

    del repo

raw_input("Check open files again")                 # files are still open
```

I tried digging deeper down the GitPython stack, but couldn't find the actual cause.

In case that's helpful, below is the same as the previous snippet, but using directly the lowest-level gitdb objects I could find to open the files:
```python
import os

import git
from gitdb.util import hex_to_bin

# Put here whatever Git repos you might have in the current folder
# Listing more than one makes the problem more visible
repos = ["ipset", "libhtp"]

raw_input("Check open files with `lsof -p %s | grep %s`" % (os.getpid(),
                                                            os.getcwd()))

raw_input("Check open files again")                 # files are still open

for name in repos:
    repo = git.Repo(name)
    sha = hex_to_bin("71acab3ca115b9ec200e440188181f6878e26f08")

    for db in repo.odb._dbs:
        try:
            for item in db._entities:               # db._entities opens *.pack files
                item[2](sha)                        # opens *.idx files

        except AttributeError as e:
            # Not all DBs have this attribute
            pass

    del repo

raw_input("Check open files again")                 # files are still open
```

But that's just where the files are open (well, in fact they are open even lower down but I didn't manage to go deeper), not where they are supposed to be closed.

issue_title: trace: using the Stackdriver exporter makes requests a lot slower(perhaps exporting is done on the same thread?)
issue_body:
Please consider this a kind post, just for the purpose of us tracking, improvement and for posterity.

I was looking at instrumenting a Python memcached client and was following along with the
code on how to dynamically intercept and instrument by method. To see the calls going through
I decided to run a sample program to help me get a better grasp of what's going on.

I noticed that just fetching from https://google.com took me 2.168.096ms yet cURL takes about 260ms, requests itself takes about the same time and so does Go
![screen shot 2018-04-21 at 3 25 48 pm](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4898263/39089284-a4275762-4578-11e8-9b16-72b2a331f27e.png)

From the trace above, after the redirect from `https://google.com` -> `https://www.google.com`, it takes Get about 1,136.115 ms when traced. However, we have all that extra time that's untraced and suspiciously perhaps that's when the trace is being exported?

Here is a sample repro
```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3

import os
import requests
import time

from opencensus.trace.samplers import always_on
from opencensus.trace.exporters import stackdriver_exporter
from opencensus.trace.exporters import print_exporter
from opencensus.trace import config_integration
from opencensus.trace.tracer import Tracer

def main():
    if os.environ.get('SD_ON'):
        # Setup the tracer and integrations
        config_integration.trace_integrations(['requests'])
        exporter = stackdriver_exporter.StackdriverExporter(project_id=os.environ.get('OPENCENSUS_PROJECT_ID', 'census-demos'))
        tracer = Tracer(sampler=always_on.AlwaysOnSampler(), exporter=exporter)
    elif os.environ.get('PE_ON'):
        config_integration.trace_integrations(['requests'])
        tracer = Tracer(sampler=always_on.AlwaysOnSampler(), exporter=print_exporter.PrintExporter())

    while True:
        url = input('URL to fetch: ')
        ts = time.time()
        res = requests.get(url)
        te = time.time()
        print(res, te-ts, ts, te)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
```

### When tracing and exporting to stackdriver
```shell
GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=~/Downloads/census-demos-237a8e1e41df.json SD_ON=True python3 fetch.py 
URL to fetch: https://google.com
<Response [200]> 3.3613481521606445 1524349962.618401 1524349965.9797492
```

### Without tracing
```shell
python3 fetch.py 
URL to fetch: https://google.com
<Response [200]> 0.2604517936706543 1524350037.002926 1524350037.263378
```

### With the printExporter
```shell
PE_ON=true python3 fetch.py 
URL to fetch: https://google.com
{'traceId': 'd5ec22743be1410c8d863e8ceff4cfd0', 'spans': [{'displayName': {'value': '[requests]get', 'truncated_byte_count': 0}, 'spanId': 9392668038562037, 'startTime': '2018-04-21T22:38:26.045212Z', 'endTime': '2018-04-21T22:38:26.305254Z', 'childSpanCount': 0, 'parentSpanId': 5452389657821971, 'attributes': {'attributeMap': {'requests/url': {'string_value': {'value': 'https://google.com', 'truncated_byte_count': 0}}, 'requests/status_code': {'string_value': {'value': '200', 'truncated_byte_count': 0}}}}}]}
{'traceId': 'd5ec22743be1410c8d863e8ceff4cfd0', 'spans': [{'displayName': {'value': '[requests]get', 'truncated_byte_count': 0}, 'spanId': 5452389657821971, 'startTime': '2018-04-21T22:38:26.044755Z', 'endTime': '2018-04-21T22:38:26.310218Z', 'childSpanCount': 0, 'attributes': {'attributeMap': {'requests/url': {'string_value': {'value': 'https://google.com', 'truncated_byte_count': 0}}, 'requests/status_code': {'string_value': {'value': '200', 'truncated_byte_count': 0}}}}}]}
<Response [200]> 0.26575613021850586 1524350306.044582 1524350306.310338
```

As we can see, the comparisons of values put things at about ~10X slower when using the Stackdriver exporter, than when not and when using the PrintExporter. Also from all that untraced/idle time, seems like that's spent doing the upload to Stackdriver. If we aren't already doing so, perhaps we could have a background process that exports to Stackdriver instead of per trace.

Thank you.

issue_title: WatsonException drops description
issue_body:
The WatsonException doesn't return the description property from the service error message. For example, with not enough training data in the Natural Language Classifier, this is returned: 

```
raise WatsonException('Error: ' + error_message + ', Code: ' + str(response.status_code))
watson_developer_cloud.watson_developer_cloud_service.WatsonException: Error: Data too small, Code: 400
```

* The description is missing from this.
* Also might want to put the message, code, and description on separate lines
* Also, to match service, labels should be lower case. For example,

```
{
   "description": "No training data is specified. The form field 'training_data' should contain the training CSV and 'training_metadata' should contain a JSON metadata string.",
   "error": "Missing training data",
   "code": 400
}
```

issue_title: Keyed helper strips keyword arguments when called in place
issue_body:
Seems like **kwargs in the helper_wrapper shadows the **kwargs from the outer scope. Renaming it allows passing random keyword arguments to the helper when called in place:

```
@wraps(helper)
def helper_wrapper(instance, *args, **kwargs2):
     return helper(key(instance), *args, **kwargs, **kwargs2)
return helper_wrapper
```
Above `**kwargs` refer to the one passed to the helper when called in place, and kwargs2 is the one passed by the datatableview. In such a scenario, one can pass random arguments in place, example:

```
open_ended = BooleanCheckmarkColumn('Open Ended', source='open_ended',
			processor=my_processor(attr='self', widget=CheckboxInput(attrs={'class': 'checkbox-primary'})))
```
Above example (duplicate ** argument) works fine in python 3.6.
